Resolve contradictions, deduplicate findings, and produce a coherent answer.\n5. **Output**: Follow the output instructions exactly as specified in your task instruction.\n\n## Sub-Agent Instructions\n- Each sub-agent works on one independent thread/perspective.\n- They should NOT see each other's work — that's the point of independent analysis.\n- Spawn approximately 6 sub-agents for the fan-out phase.\n\n\n# Django Ticket #32365: zoneinfo should be the default\n\nWe're doing the pytz -> zoneinfo migration for Django 4.0.\n\nThe codebase currently uses pytz as the default timezone implementation\nacross core utils, database backends, template tags, docs, and tests.\nWe need to flip that so zoneinfo is the default path.\n\n- zoneinfo (or backports.zoneinfo on older Python) replaces pytz everywhere.\n- pytz is no longer a hard dependency.\n- django.utils.timezone.utc should use the standard library UTC.\n- Add a transitional setting so existing projects can keep using pytz\n  temporarily. Overriding it should trigger a deprecation warning since\n  pytz support will be removed in Django 5.0.\n- The is_dst parameter should be deprecated wherever it currently appears\n  in timezone-aware functions. It should raise a deprecation warning and\n  no longer default to None.\n- Update all relevant documentation and tests.\n\nConstraints:\n\n- Work in `/testbed`.\n- Do not modify files outside the Django source tree and its tests.\n- Do not modify `tests/runtests.py` or the test runner infrastructure.\n\n\n## Gold Decomposition Guide\nsub_tasks:\n  - id: core-timezone-utils\n    description: >\n      Own /testbed/django/utils/timezone.py. Currently it imports pytz\n      unconditionally and uses pytz.utc everywhere. After your changes:\n      the file must import zoneinfo (not pytz) at module level, remove\n      any unconditional 'import pytz', set utc to datetime.timezone.utc,\n      use zoneinfo.ZoneInfo for timezone construction, and fall back to\n      pytz only when USE_DEPRECATED_PYTZ is True. The is_dst parameter\n      on make_aware() must emit a deprecation warning and its default\n      value must no longer be None.\n      Do not modify tests, docs, DB backends, or settings files.\n    depends_on: []\n    parallel_group: fan-out\n\n  - id: settings-and-config\n    description: >\n      Own /testbed/django/conf/ and /testbed/setup.cfg. In setup.cfg, remove pytz as a hard install\n      dependency and add backports.zoneinfo as a conditional dependency for\n      Python < 3.9 and tzdata for Windows.\n      Do not modify tests, docs, or other source files.\n    depends_on: []\n    parallel_group: fan-out\n\n  - id: db-backends-and-models\n    description: >\n      Own Django source files under /testbed/django/db/,\n      /testbed/django/templatetags/, and\n      /testbed/django/contrib/admin/templatetags/. Currently these layers\n      use pytz directly for timezone construction. After your changes:\n      both backends/base/base.py and backends/sqlite3/base.py should use\n      zoneinfo by default and reference USE_DEPRECATED_PYTZ for the pytz\n      fallback path — neither should have an unconditional pytz import.\n      The migration serializer (db/migrations/serializer.py) must not\n      produce pytz-specific representations like <UTC>.\n      templatetags/tz.py must import zoneinfo instead of pytz and handle\n      both ZoneInfoNotFoundError and pytz.UnknownTimeZoneError.\n      is_dst parameters in Trunc functions (db/models/functions/datetime.py)\n      and datetimes() (db/models/query.py) must no longer default to None.\n      Do not modify tests or docs.\n    depends_on: []\n    parallel_group: fan-out\n\n  - id: documentation\n    description: >\n      Own all files under /testbed/docs/. Update at minimum:\n      releases/4.0.txt, internals/deprecation.txt, ref/settings.txt,\n      topics/i18n/timezones.txt, ref/utils.txt, and\n      ref/models/database-functions.txt. After your changes, docs should\n      describe zoneinfo as the default, document USE_DEPRECATED_PYTZ, and\n      mark is_dst as deprecated. Do not modify any Python source or test\n      files.\n    depends_on: []\n    parallel_group: fan-out\n\n  - id: test-updates\n    description: >\n      Own all files under /testbed/tests/. Currently the tests assume pytz\n      is always available and is the default. After your changes, zoneinfo\n      should be the default in all tests, pytz imports should be\n      conditional, and there should be coverage for USE_DEPRECATED_PYTZ\n      and is_dst deprecation warnings. 'BigAutoField': 'AUTOINCREMENT',\n   126\t        'SmallAutoField': 'AUTOINCREMENT',\n   127\t    }\n   128\t    # SQLite requires LIKE statements to include an ESCAPE clause if the value\n   129\t    # being escaped has a percent or underscore in it.\n   130\t    # See https://www.sqlite.org/lang_expr.html for an explanation.\n   131\t    operators = {\n   132\t        'exact': '= %s',\n   133\t        'iexact': \"LIKE %s ESCAPE '\\\\'\",\n   134\t        'contains': \"LIKE %s ESCAPE '\\\\'\",\n   135\t        'icontains': \"LIKE %s ESCAPE '\\\\'\",\n   136\t        'regex': 'REGEXP %s',\n ESCAPE '\\\\'\",\n   146\t    }\n   147\t\n   148\t    # The patterns below are used to generate SQL pattern lookup clauses when\n   149\t    # the right-hand side of the lookup isn't a raw string (it When it is None, savepoints don't make sense\n   281\t        # because autocommit is enabled. The only exception is inside 'atomic'\n   282\t        # blocks. This method is intended to be used in conjunction with\n   315\t        `disable_constraint_checking()` and `enable_constraint_checking()`, to\n   316\t        determine if rows with invalid                       % self.ops.quote_name(table_name)\n   328\t                        ).fetchall()\n   329\t                        for table_name in table_names\n   330\t                    )\n ).fetchall()[foreign_key_index]\n   336\t                    column_name, referenced_column_name = foreign_key[3:5]\n   337\t                    primary_key_column_name = SQLiteCursorWrapper(Database.Cursor):\n   407\t    \"\"\"\n   408\t    Django uses \"format\" style placeholders, but pysqlite2 uses \"qmark\" style.\n   409\t    This fixes it -- but note that if you           return Database.Cursor.execute(self, query)\n   415\t        query = self.convert_query(query)\n   416\t        return Database.Cursor.execute(self, query, params)\n   417\t\n   418\t    def convert_query(self, query):\n   423\t        return FORMAT_QMARK_REGEX.sub('?', query).replace('%%', '%')\n   424\t\n   425\t\n   426\tdef _sqlite_datetime_parse(dt, tzname=None, conn_tzname=None):\n   427\t    if dt is None:\n   428\t        return None\n   429\t    try:\n   430\t        dt = backend_utils.typecast_timestamp(dt)\n   431\t    except (TypeError, ValueError):\n   432\t        return None\n   433\t    if conn_tzname:\n   434\t        dt = dt.replace(tzinfo=pytz.timezone(conn_tzname))\n   435\t    if tzname is not None and tzname != conn_tzname:\n   436\t        sign_index = tzname.find('+') + tzname.find('-') + 1\n   437\t        if sign_index > -1:\n   438\t            sign = tzname[sign_index]\n   439\t            tzname, offset = tzname.split(sign)\n   440\t += offset_delta if sign == '+' else -offset_delta\n   444\t        dt = timezone.localtime(dt, pytz.timezone(tzname))\n   445\t    return dt\n   446\t\n   447\t\n   448\tdef _sqlite_date_trunc(lookup_type, dt, tzname, conn_tzname):\n   449\t    dt = _sqlite_datetime_parse(dt, tzname, conn_tzname)\n   450\t    if dt is None:\n   451\t        return None\n   452\t    if lookup_type == 'year':\n   453\t        return \"%i-01-01\" % dt.year\n   454\t    elif lookup_type == 'quarter':\n   455\t        month_in_quarter = dt.month - (dt.month - 1) % 3\n   456\t        return '%i-%02i-01' % (dt.year, month_in_quarter)\n   457\t    elif lookup_type == 'month':\n   458\t        return \"%i-%02i-01\" % (dt.year, dt.month)\n   459\t    elif lookup_type == 'week':\n   460\t        dt = dt - datetime.timedelta(days=dt.weekday())\n   461\t        return \"%i-%02i-%02i\" % (dt.year, dt.month, dt.day)\n   462\t    elif lookup_type == 'day':\n   463\t        return \"%i-%02i-%02i\" % (dt.year, dt.month, dt.day)\n   464\t\n   465\t\n   466\tdef _sqlite_time_trunc(lookup_type, dt, tzname, conn_tzname):\n   467\t    if dt is None:\n   468\t        return None\n   469\t    dt_parsed = _sqlite_datetime_parse(dt, tzname, conn_tzname)\n   470\t    if dt_parsed is None:\n   471\t        try:\n   472\t            dt = backend_utils.typecast_time(dt)\n   473\t % (dt.hour, dt.minute, dt.second)\n   483\t\n   484\t\n   485\tdef _sqlite_datetime_cast_date(dt, tzname, conn_tzname):\n   486\t    dt = _sqlite_datetime_parse(dt, tzname, conn_tzname)\n   487\t _sqlite_datetime_extract(lookup_type, dt, tzname=None, conn_tzname=None):\n   500\t    dt = _sqlite_datetime_parse(dt, tzname, conn_tzname)\n   501\t    if dt is None:\n   502\t        return None\n   503\t    if lookup_type == 'week_day':\n   504\t        return (dt.isoweekday() % 7) + 1\n   505\t    elif lookup_type == 'iso_week_day':\n   506\t        return dt.isoweekday()\n   507\t    elif 'iso_year':\n   512\t        return dt.isocalendar()[0]\n   513\t    else:\n   514\t        return getattr(dt, lookup_type)\n   515\t\n   516\t\n   517\tdef _sqlite_datetime_trunc(lookup_type, dt, obj.save(force_insert=True, using=self.db)\n   458\t        return obj\n   459\t\n   460\t    def _prepare_for_bulk_create(self, objs):\n   461\t        for obj in objs:\n   462\t            if obj.pk obj._prepare_related_fields_for_save(operation_name='bulk_create')\n   466\t\n   467\t    def bulk_create(self, objs, batch_size=None, ignore_conflicts=False):\n   468\t        \"\"\"\n   469\t        Insert each of the instances into the database. Do *not* call\n   470\t        save() on each of the instances, do not send any pre/post_save\n   471\t        signals, and do not set the primary key attribute if it is an\n   472\t        autoincrement field (except if features.can_return_rows_from_bulk_insert=True).\n   473\t        Multi-table models are not supported.\n   474\t        \"\"\"\n   475\t        # When you bulk insert you don't get the primary keys back (if it's an\n   476\t        # autoincrement, except if can_return_rows_from_bulk_insert=True), so\n   477\t        # you can't insert into the child tables which references this. There\n   478\t        # are two workarounds:\n   479\t        # 1) This could be implemented if you didn't have an autoincrement pk\n   480\t        # 2) You could do it by doing O(n) normal inserts into the parent\n   481\t        #    tables to get the primary keys back and then doing a single bulk\n   482\t        #    insert into the childmost table.\n   483\t        # We currently set the primary keys on the objects when using\n   484\t        # PostgreSQL via the RETURNING ID clause. It should be possible for\n   485\t        # Oracle as well, but the semantics for extracting the primary keys is\n   486\t        # trickier so it's not done yet.\n   487\t        if batch_size is not None and batch_size <= 0:\n   488\t            raise ValueError('Batch size must be a positive integer.')\n   489\t        # Check that the parents share the same concrete model with the our\n   490\t        # model to detect the inheritance pattern ConcreteGrandParent ->\n   491\t        # MultiTableParent -> ProxyChild. Simply checking self.model._meta.proxy\n   492\t        # would not identify that case as involving multiple tables.\n   493\t        for parent in self.model._meta.get_parent_list():\n   494\t            if parent._meta.concrete_model is not self.model._meta.concrete_model:\n   495\t                raise ValueError(\"Can't bulk create a multi-table inherited model\")\n   496\t        if not objs:\n   497\t            return objs\n   498\t        self._for_write = True\n   499\t        connection = connections[self.db]\n   500\t        opts = self.model._meta\n   501\t        fields = opts.concrete_fields\n   502\t        objs = list(objs)\n   503\t        self._prepare_for_bulk_create(objs)\n   504\t        with transaction.atomic(using=self.db, savepoint=False):\n   505\t            objs_with_pk, objs_without_pk = partition(lambda o: o.pk is None, objs)\n   506\t            if objs_with_pk:\n   507\t                returned_columns = self._batched_insert(\n   508\t                    objs_with_pk, fields, batch_size, ignore_conflicts=ignore_conflicts,\n   509\t                )\n   510\t                for obj_with_pk, results in zip(objs_with_pk, returned_columns):\n   511\t                    for result, field in zip(results, opts.db_returning_fields):\n   512\t                        if field != opts.pk:\n   513\t                            setattr(obj_with_pk, field.attname, result)\n   514\t                for obj_with_pk in objs_with_pk:\n   515\t                    obj_with_pk._state.adding = False\n   516\t                    obj_with_pk._state.db = self.db\n   517\t            if objs_without_pk:\n   518\t                fields = [f for f in fields if not isinstance(f, AutoField)]\n   519\t                returned_columns = self._batched_insert(\n   520\t                    objs_without_pk, fields, batch_size, ignore_conflicts=ignore_conflicts,\n   521\t                )\n   522\t                if connection.features.can_return_rows_from_bulk_insert and not ignore_conflicts:\n   523\t                    assert len(returned_columns) == len(objs_without_pk)\n   524\t                for obj_without_pk, results in zip(objs_without_pk, returned_columns):\n   525\t                    for result, field in zip(results, opts.db_returning_fields):\n   526\t                        setattr(obj_without_pk, field.attname, result)\n   527\t                    obj_without_pk._state.adding = False\n   528\t                    obj_without_pk._state.db = self.db\n   529\t\n   530\t        return objs\n   531\t\n   532\t    def bulk_update(self, objs, fields, batch_size=None):\n   533\t        \"\"\"\n   534\t        Update the given fields in each of the given objects in the database.\n   535\t        \"\"\"\n   536\t        if batch_size is not None and batch_size < 0:\n   537\t            raise ValueError('Batch size must be a positive integer.')\n   538\t        if not fields:\n   539\t            raise ValueError('Field names must be given to bulk_update().')\n   540\t        objs = tuple(objs)\n   541\t        if any(obj.pk is None for obj in objs):\n   542\t            raise ValueError('All bulk_update() objects must have a primary key set.')\n   543\t        fields = [self.model._meta.get_field(name) for name in fields]\n   544\t        if any(not f.concrete or f.many_to_many for f in fields):\n   545\t            raise ValueError('bulk_update() can only be used with concrete fields.')\n   546\t        if any(f.primary_key for f in fields):\n   547\t            raise ValueError('bulk_update() cannot be used with primary key fields.')\n   548\t        if not objs:\n   549\t            return 0\n   550\t        # PK is used twice in the resulting update query, once in the filter\n   551\t        # and once in the WHEN. Each field will also have one CAST.\n   552\t        max_batch_size = connections[self.db].ops.bulk_batch_size(['pk', 'pk'] + fields, objs)\n   553\t        batch_size = min(batch_size, max_batch_size) if batch_size else max_batch_size\n   554\t        requires_casting = connections[self.db].features.requires_casted_case_in_updates\n   555\t        batches = (objs[i:i + batch_size] for i in range(0, len(objs), batch_size))\n   556\t        updates = []\n   557\t        for batch_objs in batches:\n   558\t            update_kwargs = {}\n   559\t            for field in fields:\n   560\t                when_statements = []\n   561\t                for obj in batch_objs:\n   562\t                    attr = getattr(obj, field.attname)\n   563\t                    if not isinstance(attr, Expression):\n   564\t                        attr = Value(attr, output_field=field)\n   565\t                    when_statements.append(When(pk=obj.pk, then=attr))\n   566\t                case_statement = Case(*when_statements, output_field=field)\n   567\t                if requires_casting:\n   568\t                    case_statement = Cast(case_statement, output_field=field)\n   569\t                update_kwargs[field.attname] = case_statement\n   570\t            updates.append(([obj.pk for obj in batch_objs], update_kwargs))\n   571\t        rows_updated = 0\n   572\t        with transaction.atomic(using=self.db, savepoint=False):\n   573\t            for pks, update_kwargs in updates:\n   574\t                rows_updated += self.filter(pk__in=pks).update(**update_kwargs)\n   575\t        return rows_updated\n   576\t    bulk_update.alters_data = True\n   577\t\n   578\t    def get_or_create(self, defaults=None, **kwargs):\n   579\t        \"\"\"\n   580\t        Look up an object with the given kwargs, creating one if necessary.\n   581\t        Return a tuple of (object, created), where created is a boolean\n   582\t        specifying whether an object was created.\n   583\t        \"\"\"\n   584\t        # The get() needs to be targeted at the write database in order\n   585\t        # to avoid potential transaction consistency problems.\n   586\t        self._for_write = True\n   587\t        try:\n   588\t            return self.get(**kwargs), False\n   589\t        except self.model.DoesNotExist:\n   590\t            params = self._extract_model_params(defaults, **kwargs)\n   591\t            # Try to create an object using passed params.\n   592\t            try:\n   593\t                with transaction.atomic(using=self.db):\n   594\t                    params = dict(resolve_callables(params))\n   595\t                    return self.create(**params), True\n   596\t            except IntegrityError:\n   597\t                try:\n   598\t                    return self.get(**kwargs), False\n   599\t                except self.model.DoesNotExist:\n   600\t                    pass\n   601\t                raise\n   602\t\n   603\t    def update_or_create(self, defaults=None, **kwargs):\n   604\t        \"\"\"\n   605\t        Look up an object with the given kwargs, updating one with defaults\n   606\t        if it exists, otherwise create a new one.\n   607\t        Return a tuple (object, created), where created is a boolean\n   608\t        specifying whether an object was created.\n   609\t        \"\"\"\n   610\t        defaults = defaults or {}\n   611\t        self._for_write = True\n   612\t        with transaction.atomic(using=self.db):\n   613\t            # Lock the row so that a concurrent update is blocked until\n   614\t            # update_or_create() has performed its save.\n   615\t            obj, created = self.select_for_update().get_or_create(defaults, **kwargs)\n   616\t            if created:\n   617\t                return obj, created\n   618\t            for k, v in _extract_model_params(self, defaults, **kwargs):\n   624\t        \"\"\"\n   625\t        Prepare `params` for creating a model instance based on the given\n   626\t        kwargs; for use by get_or_create().\n   627\t        \"\"\"\n   628\t        defaults = defaults or {}\n   629\t        params = {k: v for k, v in kwargs.items() if LOOKUP_SEP not in k}\n   630\t        params.update(defaults)\n   631\t        property_names = self.model._meta._property_names\n   632\t        invalid_params = []\n   633\t        for param in params:\n   634\t            try:\n   635\t                self.model._meta.get_field(param)\n   636\t            except exceptions.FieldDoesNotExist:\n   637\t                # It's okay to use a model's property if it has a setter.\n   638\t                if not (param in property_names and getattr(self.model, param).fset):\n   639\t                    invalid_params.append(param)\n   640\t        if invalid_params:\n   641\t \"', '\".join(sorted(invalid_params)),\n   645\t                ))\n   646\t        return params\n   647\t\n   648\t    def _earliest(self, *fields):\n   649\t        \"\"\"\n   650\t        Return self.reverse()._earliest(*fields)\n   679\t\n   680\t    def first(self):\n   681\t        \"\"\"Return the first object of a query or None if no match is found.\"\"\"\n   682\t        for obj in is found.\"\"\"\n   687\t        for obj in (self.reverse() if self.ordered else self.order_by('-pk'))[:1]:\n   688\t            return obj\n   689\t\n   690\t    def in_bulk(self, id_list=None, *, field_name='pk'):\n   691\t        \"\"\"\n   692\t        Return a dictionary mapping each of the given IDs to the object with\n   693\t        that ID. If `id_list` isn't provided, evaluate the entire QuerySet.\n   694\t        \"\"\"\n   695\t        if self.query.is_sliced:\n   696\t            raise TypeError(\"Cannot use 'limit' or 'offset' with in_bulk().\")\n   697\t        opts = self.model._meta\n   698\t        unique_fields = [\n   699\t            constraint.fields[0]\n   700\t            for constraint in opts.total_unique_constraints\n   701\t            if len(constraint.fields) == 1\n   702\t        ]\n   703\t        if (\n   704\t            field_name != 'pk' and\n   705\t            not opts.get_field(field_name).unique and\n   706\t            field_name not in unique_fields and\n   707\t            self.query.distinct_fields != (field_name,)\n   708\t        ):\n   709\t            raise ValueError(\"in_bulk()'s field_name must be a unique field but %r isn't.\" % field_name)\n   710\t        if id_list is not None:\n   711\t            if not id_list:\n   712\t                return {}\n   713\t            filter_key = '{}__in'.format(field_name)\n   714\t            batch_size = connections[self.db].features.max_query_params\n   715\t            id_list = tuple(id_list)\n   716\t            # 779\t        fields to the appropriate values.\n   780\t        \"\"\"\n   781\t        self._not_support_combined_queries('update')\n   782\t        if self.query.is_sliced:\n   783\t 858\t    def _values(self, *fields, **expressions):\n   859\t        clone = self._chain()\n   860\t        if expressions:\n   861\t            clone = clone.annotate(**expressions)\n   862\t values_list(self, *fields, flat=False, named=False):\n   873\t        if flat and named:\n   874\t            raise TypeError(\"'flat' and 'named' can't be used together.\")\n   875\t        if flat and len(fields) > 1:\n   876\t            raise TypeError(\"'flat' is not valid when values_list is called with more than one field.\")\n   877\t\n   878\t        field_names = {f for f in fields if not hasattr(f, 'resolve_expression')}\n   879\t        _fields = []\n   880\t        expressions = {}\n   881\t        counter = 1\n   882\t        for field in fields:\n   883\t            if hasattr(field, 'resolve_expression'):\n   884\t                field_id_prefix = getattr(field, 'default_alias', field.__class__.__name__.lower())\n   885\t                while True:\n   886\t 890\t                expressions[field_id] = field\n   891\t                _fields.append(field_id)\n   892\t            else:\n   893\t                _fields.append(field)\n   894\t\n   895\t {\n   429\t            'dt__min': datetime.datetime(2011, 9, 1, 3, 20, 40, tzinfo=EAT),\n   430\t            'dt__max': datetime.datetime(2011, 9, 1, 23, 20, 20, tzinfo=EAT),\n   431\t        })\n self.assertEqual(event.dt, initial_dt + timedelta(hours=2))\n   594\t\n   595\t\n   596\t@override_settings(TIME_ZONE='Africa/Nairobi', USE_TZ=True)\n   597\tclass ForcedTimeZoneDatabaseTests(TransactionTestCase):\n   598\t    \"\"\"\n   599\t    Test the TIME_ZONE database configuration parameter.\n   600\t\n   601\t    Since this involves reading and writing to the same database through two\n   602\t    connections, this is a TransactionTestCase.\n   603\t    \"\"\"\n   604\t\n   605\t    available_apps = ['timezones']\n   606\t\n   607\t    @classmethod\n   608\t    def setUpClass(cls):\n   609\t        # @skipIfDBFeature and @skipUnlessDBFeature cannot be chained. The\n   610\t        # outermost takes precedence. Handle skipping manually instead.\n   611\t        if connection.features.supports_timezones:\n   612\t            raise SkipTest(\"Database has feature(s) supports_timezones\")\n   613\t        if not connection.features.test_db_allows_multiple_connections:\n   614\t            raise SkipTest(\"Database doesn't support feature(s): test_db_allows_multiple_connections\")\n   615\t\n   616\t        super().setUpClass()\n   617\t\n   618\t    def test_read_datetime(self):\n   619\t        fake_dt = datetime.datetime(2011, 9, 1, 17, 20, 30, tzinfo=UTC)\n   620\t        Event.objects.create(dt=fake_dt)\n   621\t\n   622\t        with override_database_connection_timezone('Asia/Bangkok'):\n   623\t            event = Event.objects.get()\n   624\t            dt = datetime.datetime(2011, 9, 1, 10, 20, 30, tzinfo=UTC)\n   625\t        self.assertEqual(event.dt, dt)\n   626\t\n   627\t    def test_write_datetime(self):\n   628\t        dt = datetime.datetime(2011, 9, 1, 10, 20, 30, tzinfo=UTC)\n   629\t        with override_database_connection_timezone('Asia/Bangkok'):\n   630\t            Event.objects.create(dt=dt)\n   631\t\n   632\t        event = Event.objects.get()\n   633\t        fake_dt = datetime.datetime(2011, 9, 1, 17, 20, 30, tzinfo=UTC)\n   634\t        self.assertEqual(event.dt, fake_dt)\n   635\t\n   636\t\n   637\t@override_settings(TIME_ZONE='Africa/Nairobi')\n   638\tclass SerializationTests(SimpleTestCase):\n   639\t\n   640\t    # Backend-specific notes:\n   641\t    # - JSON supports only milliseconds, microseconds will be truncated.\n   642\t    # - PyYAML dumps the UTC offset correctly for timezone-aware datetimes.\n   643\t    #   When PyYAML < 5.3 loads this representation, it subtracts the offset\n   644\t    #   and returns a naive datetime object in UTC. PyYAML 5.3+ loads timezones\n   645\t    #   correctly.\n   646\t    # Tests are adapted to take these quirks into account.\n   647\t\n   648\t    def assert_python_contains_datetime(self, objects, dt):\n   649\t        self.assertEqual(objects[0]['fields']['dt'], dt)\n   650\t\n   651\t    def assert_json_contains_datetime(self, json, dt):\n   652\t        self.assertIn('\"fields\": {\"dt\": \"%s\"}' % dt, json)\n   653\t\n   654\t    def assert_xml_contains_datetime(self, xml, dt):\n   655\t        field = parseString(xml).getElementsByTagName('field')[0]\n   656\t        self.assertXMLEqual(field.childNodes[0].wholeText, dt)\n   657\t\n   658\t    def assert_yaml_contains_datetime(self, yaml, dt):\n   659\t        # Depending on the yaml dumper, '!timestamp' might be absent\n   660\t        self.assertRegex(yaml, r\"\\n  fields: {dt: !(!timestamp)? '%s'}\" % re.escape(dt))\n   661\t\n   662\t    def test_naive_datetime(self):\n   663\t        dt = datetime.datetime(2011, 9, 1, 13, 20, 30)\n   664\t\n   665\t        data = serializers.serialize('python', [Event(dt=dt)])\n   666\t        self.assert_python_contains_datetime(data, dt)\n   667\t        obj = next(serializers.deserialize('python', data)).object\n   668\t        self.assertEqual(obj.dt, dt)\n   669\t\n   670\t        data = serializers.serialize('json', [Event(dt=dt)])\n   671\t        self.assert_json_contains_datetime(data, \"2011-09-01T13:20:30\")\n   672\t        obj = next(serializers.deserialize('json', data)).object\n   673\t        self.assertEqual(obj.dt, dt)\n   674\t\n   675\t        data = serializers.serialize('xml', [Event(dt=dt)])\n   676\t        self.assert_xml_contains_datetime(data, \"2011-09-01T13:20:30\")\n   677\t        obj = next(serializers.deserialize('xml', data)).object\n   678\t        self.assertEqual(obj.dt, dt)\n   679\t\n   680\t        if not isinstance(serializers.get_serializer('yaml'), serializers.BadSerializer):\n   681\t            data = serializers.serialize('yaml', [Event(dt=dt)], default_flow_style=None)\n   682\t            self.assert_yaml_contains_datetime(data, \"2011-09-01 13:20:30\")\n   683\t            obj = next(serializers.deserialize('yaml', data)).object\n   684\t            self.assertEqual(obj.dt, dt)\n   685\t\n   686\t    def test_naive_datetime_with_microsecond(self):\n   687\t        dt = datetime.datetime(2011, 9, 1, 13, 20, 30, 405060)\n   688\t\n   689\t        data = serializers.serialize('python', [Event(dt=dt)])\n   690\t        self.assert_python_contains_datetime(data, dt)\n   691\t        obj = next(serializers.deserialize('python', data)).object\n   692\t        self.assertEqual(obj.dt, dt)\n   693\t\n   694\t        data = serializers.serialize('json', [Event(dt=dt)])\n   695\t        self.assert_json_contains_datetime(data, \"2011-09-01T13:20:30.405\")\n   696\t        obj = next(serializers.deserialize('json', data)).object\n   697\t        self.assertEqual(obj.dt, dt.replace(microsecond=405000))\n   698\t\n   699\t serializers.BadSerializer):\n   705\t            data = serializers.serialize('yaml', [Event(dt=dt)], default_flow_style=None)\n   706\t            self.assert_yaml_contains_datetime(data, \"2011-09-01 13:20:30.405060\")\n   707\t            obj = next(serializers.deserialize('yaml', data)).object\n   708\t            self.assertEqual(obj.dt, dt)\n   709\t\n   710\t    def test_aware_datetime_with_microsecond(self):\n   711\t        dt = datetime.datetime(2011, 9, 1, 17, 20, 30, 405060, tzinfo=ICT)\n   712\t\n   713\t        data = serializers.serialize('python',         else:\n   735\t                self.assertEqual(obj.dt, dt)\n   736\t\n   737\t    def test_aware_datetime_in_utc(self):\n   738\t        dt = datetime.datetime(2011, 9, 1, 10, 20, 30, However, it's not the default as it requires OpenSSL\n    66\t1.1+ and more memory.\n    67\t\n    68\tRedis cache backend\n    69\t-------------------\n    70\t\n    71\tThe new ``django.core.cache.backends.redis.RedisCache`` cache backend provides\n    72\tbuilt-in support for caching with Redis. `redis-py`_ 3.0.0 or higher is\n    73\trequired. For more details, see the :ref:`documentation on caching with Redis\n    74\tin Django <redis>`.\n    75\t\n    76\t.. _`redis-py`: https://pypi.org/project/redis/\n    77\t\n    78\tMinor features\n 85\t  contains the admin site header.\n    86\t\n    87\t* The new :meth:`.ModelAdmin.get_formset_kwargs` method allows customizing the\n    88\t  keyword arguments passed to the constructor of a formset.\n    89\t\n    90\t* The navigation sidebar now has a quick filter toolbar.\n    91\t\n    92\t* The new context variable ``model`` which contains the model class for each\n    93\t  model is added to the :meth:`.AdminSite.each_context` method.\n    94\t\n    95\t* The new :attr:`.ModelAdmin.search_help_text` attribute allows specifying a\n    96\t  descriptive text for the search box.\n    97\t\n    98\t* The :attr:`.InlineModelAdmin.verbose_name_plural` attribute now fallbacks to\n    99\t  the :attr:`.InlineModelAdmin.verbose_name` + ``'s'``.\n   100\t\n iteration count for the PBKDF2 password hasher is increased from\n   113\t  260,000 to 320,000.\n   114\t\n   115\t* The new\n   116\t  :attr:`LoginView.next_page <django.contrib.auth.views.LoginView.next_page>`\n   117\t  attribute and\n   118\t  :meth:`~django.contrib.auth.views.LoginView.get_default_redirect_url` method\n   119\t  allow customizing the redirect after login.\n   120\t\n   121\t:mod:`django.contrib.contenttypes`\n   122\t~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   123\t\n   124\t* ...\n   125\t\n   126\t:mod:`django.contrib.gis`\n   127\t~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   128\t\n   129\t* Added support for SpatiaLite 5.\n   130\t\n   131\t* :class:`~django.contrib.gis.gdal.GDALRaster` now allows creating rasters in\n   132\t  any GDAL virtual filesystem.\n   133\t\n   134\t:mod:`django.contrib.messages`\n   135\t~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   136\t\n   137\t* ...\n   138\t\n   139\t:mod:`django.contrib.postgres`\n   140\t~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   141\t\n   142\t* The PostgreSQL backend now supports connecting by a service name. See\n   143\t  :ref:`postgresql-connection-settings` for more details.\n   144\t\n   145\t* The new :class:`~django.contrib.postgres.operations.AddConstraintNotValid`\n   146\t  operation allows creating check constraints on PostgreSQL without verifying\n   147\t  that all existing rows satisfy the new constraint.\n   148\t\n   149\t* The new :class:`~django.contrib.postgres.operations.ValidateConstraint`\n   150\t  operation allows validating check constraints which were created using\n   151\t  :class:`~django.contrib.postgres.operations.AddConstraintNotValid` on\n   152\t  PostgreSQL.\n   153\t\n   154\t* The new\n   155\t  :class:`ArraySubquery() <django.contrib.postgres.expressions.ArraySubquery>`\n   156\t  expression allows using subqueries to construct lists of values on\n   157\t  PostgreSQL.\n   158\t\n   159\t:mod:`django.contrib.redirects`\n   160\t~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   161\t\n   162\t* ...\n   163\t\n   164\t:mod:`django.contrib.sessions`\n   165\t~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   166\t\n   167\t* ...\n   168\t\n   169\t:mod:`django.contrib.sitemaps`\n   170\t~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   171\t\n   172\t* ...\n   173\t\n   174\t:mod:`django.contrib.sites`\n   175\t~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   176\t\n   177\t* ...\n   178\t\n   179\t:mod:`django.contrib.staticfiles`\n   180\t~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   181\t\n   182\t* :class:`~django.contrib.staticfiles.storage.ManifestStaticFilesStorage` now\n   183\t  replaces paths to JavaScript source map references with their hashed\n   184\t  counterparts.\n   185\t\n   186\t* :class:`~django.contrib.staticfiles.storage.ManifestStaticFilesStorage` now\n   187\t  replaces paths to JavaScript modules in ``import`` and ``export`` statements\n   188\t  with their hashed counterparts.\n   189\t\n   190\t* The new ``manifest_storage`` argument of\n   191\t  :class:`~django.contrib.staticfiles.storage.ManifestFilesMixin` and\n   192\t  :class:`~django.contrib.staticfiles.storage.ManifestStaticFilesStorage`\n   193\t  allows customizing the manifest file storage.\n   194\t\n   195\t:mod:`django.contrib.syndication`\n   196\t~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   197\t\n   198\t* ...\n   199\t\n   200\tCache\n   201\t~~~~~\n   202\t\n   203\t* The new async API for ``django.core.cache.backends.base.BaseCache`` begins\n   204\t  the process of making cache backends async-compatible. The new async methods\n   205\t  all have ``a`` prefixed names, e.g. ``aadd()``, ``aget()``, ``aset()``,\n   206\t  ``aget_or_set()``, or ``adelete_many()``.\n   207\t\n   208\t  Going forward, the ``a`` prefix will be used for async variants of methods\n   209\t  generally.\n   210\t\n   211\tCSRF\n   212\t~~~~\n   213\t\n   214\t* CSRF protection now consults the ``Origin`` header, if present. To facilitate\n   215\t  this, :ref:`some changes <csrf-trusted-origins-changes-4.0>` to the\n   216\t  :setting:`CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS` setting are required.\n   217\t\n   218\tDecorators\n   219\t~~~~~~~~~~\n   220\t\n   221\t* ...\n   222\t\n   223\tEmail\n   224\t~~~~~\n   225\t\n   226\t* ...\n   227\t\n   228\tError Reporting\n   229\t~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   230\t\n   231\t* ...\n   232\t\n   233\tFile Storage\n   234\t~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   235\t\n   236\t* ...\n   237\t\n   238\tFile Uploads\n   239\t~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   240\t\n   241\t* ...\n   242\t\n   243\tForms\n   244\t~~~~~\n   245\t\n   246\t* :class:`~django.forms.ModelChoiceField` now includes the provided value in\n   247\t  the ``params`` argument of a raised\n   248\t  :exc:`~django.core.exceptions.ValidationError` for the ``invalid_choice``\n   249\t  error message. This allows custom error messages to use the ``%(value)s``\n   250\t  placeholder.\n   251\t\n   252\t* :class:`~django.forms.formsets.BaseFormSet` now renders non-form errors with\n   253\t  an additional class of ``nonform`` to help distinguish them from\n   254\t  form-specific errors.\n   255\t\n   256\t* :class:`~django.forms.formsets.BaseFormSet` now allows customizing the widget\n   257\t  used when deleting forms via\n   258\t  :attr:`~django.forms.formsets.BaseFormSet.can_delete` by setting the\n   259\t  :attr:`~django.forms.formsets.BaseFormSet.deletion_widget` attribute or\n   260\t  overriding :meth:`~django.forms.formsets.BaseFormSet.get_deletion_widget`\n   261\t  method.\n   262\t\n   263\tGeneric Views\n   264\t~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   265\t\n   266\t* :class:`~django.views.generic.edit.DeleteView` now uses\n   267\t  :class:`~django.views.generic.edit.FormMixin`, allowing you to provide a\n   268\t  :class:`~django.forms.Form` subclass, with a checkbox for example, to confirm\n   269\t  deletion. In addition, this allows ``DeleteView`` to function with\n   270\t  :class:`django.contrib.messages.views.SuccessMessageMixin`.\n   271\t\n   272\tInternationalization\n   273\t~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   274\t\n   275\t* ...\n   276\t\n   277\tLogging\n   278\t~~~~~~~\n   279\t\n   280\t* The alias of the database used in an SQL call is now passed as extra context\n   281\t  along with each message to the :ref:`django-db-logger` logger.\n   282\t\n   283\tManagement Commands\n   284\t~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   285\t\n   286\t* The :djadmin:`runserver` management command now supports the\n   287\t  :option:`--skip-checks` option.\n   288\t\n   289\t* On PostgreSQL, :djadmin:`dbshell` now supports specifying a password file.\n   290\t\n   291\t* The :djadmin:`shell` command now respects :py:data:`sys.__interactivehook__`\n   292\t  at startup. This allows loading shell history between interactive sessions.\n   293\t  As a consequence, ``readline`` is no longer loaded if running in *isolated*\n   294\t  mode.\n   295\t\n   296\t* The new :attr:`BaseCommand.suppressed_base_arguments\n   297\t  <django.core.management.BaseCommand.suppressed_base_arguments>` attribute\n   298\t  allows suppressing unsupported default command options in the help output.\n   299\t\n   300\t* The new :option:`startapp --exclude` and :option:`startproject --exclude`\n   301\t  options allow excluding directories from the template.\n   302\t\n   303\tMigrations\n   304\t~~~~~~~~~~\n   305\t\n   306\t* ...\n   307\t\n   308\tModels\n   309\t~~~~~~\n   310\t\n   311\t* New :meth:`QuerySet.contains(obj) <.QuerySet.contains>` method returns\n   312\t  whether the queryset contains the given object. This tries to perform the\n   313\t  query in the simplest and fastest way possible.\n   314\t\n   315\t* The new ``precision`` argument of the\n   316\t  :class:`Round() <django.db.models.functions.Round>` database function allows\n   317\t  specifying the number of decimal places after rounding.\n   318\t\n   319\t* :meth:`.QuerySet.bulk_create` now sets the primary key on objects when using\n   320\t  SQLite 3.35+.\n   321\t\n   322\t* :class:`~django.db.models.DurationField` now supports multiplying and\n   323\t  dividing by scalar values on SQLite.\n   324\t\n   325\t* :meth:`.QuerySet.bulk_update` now returns the number of objects updated.\n   326\t\n   327\t* The new :attr:`.Aggregate.empty_aggregate_value` attribute allows specifying\n   328\t  a value to return when the aggregation is used over an empty result set.\n   329\t\n   330\t* The ``skip_locked`` argument of :meth:`.QuerySet.select_for_update()` is now\n   331\t  allowed on MariaDB 10.6+.\n   332\t\n   333\t* :class:`~django.db.models.Lookup` expressions may now be used in ``QuerySet``\n   334\t  annotations, aggregations, and directly in filters.\n   335\t\n   336\t* The new :ref:`default <aggregate-default>` argument for built-in aggregates\n   337\t  allows specifying a value to be returned when the queryset (or grouping)\n   338\t  contains no entries, rather than ``None``.\n   339\t\n   340\tRequests and Responses\n   341\t~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   342\t\n   343\t* The :class:`~django.middleware.security.SecurityMiddleware` now adds the\n   344\t  :ref:`Cross-Origin Opener Policy <cross-origin-opener-policy>` header with a\n   345\t  value of ``'same-origin'`` to prevent cross-origin popups from sharing the\n   346\t  same browsing context. You can prevent this header from being added by\n   347\t  setting the :setting:`SECURE_CROSS_ORIGIN_OPENER_POLICY` setting to ``None``.\n   348\t\n   349\tSecurity\n   350\t~~~~~~~~\n   351\t\n   352\t* ...\n   353\t\n   354\tSerialization\n   355\t~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   356\t\n   357\t* ...\n   358\t\n   359\tSignals\n   360\t~~~~~~~\n   361\t\n   362\t* The new ``stdout`` argument for :func:`~django.db.models.signals.pre_migrate`\n   363\t  and :func:`~django.db.models.signals.post_migrate` signals allows redirecting\n   364\t  output to a stream-like object. It should be preferred over\n   365\t  :py:data:`sys.stdout` and :py:func:`print` when emitting verbose output in\n   366\t  order to allow proper capture when testing.\n   367\t\n   368\tTemplates\n   369\t~~~~~~~~~\n   370\t\n   371\t* :tfilter:`floatformat` template filter now allows using the ``u`` suffix to\n   372\t  force disabling localization.\n   373\t\n   374\tTests\n   375\t~~~~~\n   376\t\n   377\t* The new ``serialized_aliases`` argument of\n   378\t  :func:`django.test.utils.setup_databases` determines which\n   379\t  :setting:`DATABASES` aliases test databases should have their state\n   380\t  serialized to allow usage of the\n   381\t  :ref:`serialized_rollback <test-case-serialized-rollback>` feature.\n   382\t\n   383\t* Django test runner now supports a :option:`--buffer <test --buffer>` option\n   384\t  with parallel tests.\n   385\t\n   386\t* The new ``logger`` argument to :class:`~django.test.runner.DiscoverRunner`\n   387\t  allows a Python :py:ref:`logger <logger>` to be used for logging.\n   388\t\n   389\t* The new :meth:`.DiscoverRunner.log` method provides a way to log messages\n   390\t  that uses the ``DiscoverRunner.logger``, or prints to the console if not set.\n   391\t\n   392\t* Django test runner now supports a :option:`--shuffle <test --shuffle>` option\n   393\t  to execute tests in a random order.\n   394\t\n   395\t* The :option:`test --parallel` option now supports the value ``auto`` to run\n   396\t  one test process for each processor core.\n   397\t\n   398\t* :meth:`.TestCase.captureOnCommitCallbacks` now captures new callbacks added\n   399\t  while executing :func:`.transaction.on_commit` callbacks.\n   400\t\n   401\tURLs\n   402\t~~~~\n   403\t\n   404\t* ...\n   405\t\n   406\tUtilities\n   407\t~~~~~~~~~\n   408\t\n   409\t* ...\n   410\t\n   411\tValidators\n   412\t~~~~~~~~~~\n   413\t\n   414\t* ...\n   415\t\n   416\t.. _backwards-incompatible-4.0:\n   417\t\n   418\tBackwards incompatible changes in 4.0\n   419\t=====================================\n   420\t\n   421\tDatabase backend API\n   422\t--------------------\n   423\t\n   424\tThis section describes changes that may be needed in third-party database\n   425\tbackends.\n   426\t\n   427\t* ``DatabaseOperations.year_lookup_bounds_for_date_field()`` and\n   428\t  ``year_lookup_bounds_for_datetime_field()`` methods now take the optional\n   429\t  ``iso_year`` argument in order to support bounds for ISO-8601 week-numbering\n   430\t  years.\n   431\t\n   432\t* The second argument of ``DatabaseSchemaEditor._unique_sql()`` and\n   433\t  ``_create_unique_sql()`` methods is now fields instead of columns.\n   434\t\n   435\t:mod:`django.contrib.gis`\n   436\t-------------------------\n   437\t\n   438\t* Support for PostGIS 2.3 is removed.\n   439\t\n   440\t* Support for GDAL 2.0 and GEOS 3.5 is removed.\n   441\t\n   442\tDropped support for PostgreSQL 9.6\n   443\t----------------------------------\n   444\t\n   445\tUpstream support for PostgreSQL 9.6 ends in November 2021. Django 4.0 supports\n   446\tPostgreSQL 10 and higher.\n   447\t\n   448\tDropped support for Oracle 12.2 and 18c\n   449\t---------------------------------------\n   450\t\n   451\tUpstream support for Oracle 12.2 ends in March 2022 and for Oracle 18c it ends\n   452\tin June 2021. Django 3.2 will be supported until April 2024. Django 4.0\n   453\tofficially supports Oracle 19c.\n   454\t\n   455\t.. _csrf-trusted-origins-changes-4.0:\n   456\t\n   457\t``CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS`` changes\n   458\t--------------------------------\n   459\t\n   460\tFormat change\n   461\t~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   462\t\n   463\tValues in the :setting:`CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS` setting must include the scheme\n   464\t(e.g. ``'http://'`` or ``'https://'``) instead of only the hostname.\n   465\t\n   466\tAlso, values that started with a dot, must now also include an asterisk before\n   467\tthe dot. For example, change ``'.example.com'`` to ``'https://*.example.com'``.\n   468\t\n   469\tA system check detects any required changes.\n   470\t\n   471\tConfiguring it may now be required\n   472\t~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   473\t\n   474\tAs CSRF protection now consults the ``Origin`` header, you may need to set\n   475\t:setting:`CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS`, particularly if you allow requests from\n   476\tsubdomains by setting :setting:`CSRF_COOKIE_DOMAIN` (or\n   477\t:setting:`SESSION_COOKIE_DOMAIN` if :setting:`CSRF_USE_SESSIONS` is enabled) to\n   478\ta value starting with a dot.\n   479\t\n   480\t``SecurityMiddleware`` no longer sets the ``X-XSS-Protection`` header\n   481\t---------------------------------------------------------------------\n   482\t\n   483\tThe :class:`~django.middleware.security.SecurityMiddleware` no longer sets the\n   484\t``X-XSS-Protection`` header if the ``SECURE_BROWSER_XSS_FILTER`` setting is\n   485\t``True``. The setting is removed.\n   486\t\n   487\tMost modern browsers don't honor the ``X-XSS-Protection`` HTTP header. You can\n   488\tuse Content-Security-Policy_ without allowing ``'unsafe-inline'`` scripts\n   489\tinstead.\n   490\t\n   491\tIf you want to support legacy browsers and set the header, use this line in a\n   492\tcustom middleware::\n   493\t\n   494\t    response.headers.setdefault('X-XSS-Protection', '1; mode=block')\n   495\t\n   496\t.. _Content-Security-Policy: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Content-Security-Policy\n   497\t\n   498\tMigrations autodetector changes\n   499\t-------------------------------\n   500\t\n   501\tThe migrations autodetector now uses model states instead of model classes.\n   502\tAlso, migration operations for ``ForeignKey`` and ``ManyToManyField`` fields no\n   503\tlonger specify attributes which were not passed to the fields during\n   504\tinitialization.\n   505\t\n   506\tAs a side-effect, running ``makemigrations`` might generate no-op\n   507\t``AlterField`` operations for ``ManyToManyField`` and ``ForeignKey`` fields in\n   508\tsome cases.\n   509\t\n   510\tMiscellaneous\n   511\t-------------\n   512\t\n   513\t* Support for ``cx_Oracle`` < 7.0 is removed.\n   514\t\n   515\t* To allow serving a Django site on a subpath without changing the value of\n   516\t  :setting:`STATIC_URL`, the leading slash is removed from that setting (now\n   517\t  ``'static/'``) in the default :djadmin:`startproject` template.\n   518\t\n   519\t* The :class:`~django.contrib.admin.AdminSite` method for the admin ``index``\n   520\t  view is no longer decorated with ``never_cache`` when accessed directly,\n   521\t  rather than via the recommended ``AdminSite.urls`` property, or\n   522\t  ``AdminSite.get_urls()`` method.\n   523\t\n   524\t* Unsupported operations on a sliced queryset now raise ``TypeError`` instead\n   525\t  of ``AssertionError``.\n   526\t\n   527\t* The undocumented ``django.test.runner.reorder_suite()`` function is renamed\n   528\t  to ``reorder_tests()``. It now accepts an iterable of tests rather than a\n   529\t  test suite, and returns an iterator of tests.\n   530\t\n   531\t* Calling ``FileSystemStorage.delete()`` with an empty ``name`` now raises\n   532\t  ``ValueError`` instead of ``AssertionError``.\n   533\t\n   534\t* Calling ``EmailMultiAlternatives.attach_alternative()`` or\n   535\t  ``EmailMessage.attach()`` with an invalid ``content`` or ``mimetype``\n   536\t  arguments now raise ``ValueError`` instead of ``AssertionError``.\n   537\t\n   538\t* :meth:`~django.test.SimpleTestCase.assertHTMLEqual` no longer considers a\n   539\t  non-boolean attribute without a value equal to an attribute with the same\n   540\t  name and value.\n   541\t\n   542\t* Tests that fail to load, for example due to syntax errors, now always match\n   543\t  when using :option:`test --tag`.\n   544\t\n   545\t* The undocumented ``django.contrib.admin.utils.lookup_needs_distinct()``\n   546\t  function is renamed to ``lookup_spawns_duplicates()``.\n   547\t\n   548\t* The undocumented ``HttpRequest.get_raw_uri()`` method is removed. The\n   549\t  :meth:`.HttpRequest.build_absolute_uri` method may be a suitable alternative.\n   550\t\n   551\t* The ``object`` argument of undocumented ``ModelAdmin.log_addition()``,\n   552\t  ``log_change()``, and ``log_deletion()`` methods is renamed to ``obj``.\n   553\t\n   554\t* :class:`~django.utils.feedgenerator.RssFeed`,\n   555\t  :class:`~django.utils.feedgenerator.Atom1Feed`, and their subclasses now\n   556\t  emit elements with no content as self-closing tags.\n   557\t\n   558\t* ``NodeList.render()`` no longer casts the output of ``render()`` method for\n   559\t  individual nodes to a string. ``Node.render()`` should always return a string\n   560\t  as documented.\n   561\t\n   562\t* The ``where_class`` property of ``django.db.models.sql.query.Query`` and the\n   563\t  ``where_class`` argument to the private ``get_extra_restriction()`` method of\n   564\t  ``ForeignObject`` and ``ForeignObjectRel`` are removed. If needed, initialize\n   565\t  ``django.db.models.sql.where.WhereNode`` instead.\n   566\t\n   567\t* The ``filter_clause`` argument of the undocumented ``Query.add_filter()``\n   568\t  method is replaced by two positional arguments ``filter_lhs`` and\n   569\t  ``filter_rhs``.\n   570\t\n   571\t* :class:`~django.middleware.csrf.CsrfViewMiddleware` now uses\n   572\t  ``request.META['CSRF_COOKIE_NEEDS_UPDATE']`` in place of\n   573\t  ``request.META['CSRF_COOKIE_USED']``, ``request.csrf_cookie_needs_reset``,\n   574\t  and ``response.csrf_cookie_set`` to track whether the CSRF cookie should be\n   575\t  sent. This is an undocumented, private API.\n   576\t\n   577\t* The undocumented ``TRANSLATOR_COMMENT_MARK`` constant is moved from\n   578\t  ``django.template.base`` to ``django.utils.translation.template``.\n   579\t\n   580\t* The ``real_apps`` argument of the undocumented\n   581\t  ``django.db.migrations.state.ProjectState.__init__()`` method must now be a\n   582\t  set if provided.\n   583\t\n   584\t* :class:`~django.forms.RadioSelect` and\n   585\t  :class:`~django.forms.CheckboxSelectMultiple` widgets are now rendered in\n   586\t  ``<div>`` tags so they are announced more concisely by screen readers. If you\n   587\t  need the previous behavior, :ref:`override the widget template\n   588\t  <overriding-built-in-widget-templates>` with the appropriate template from\n   589\t  Django 3.2.\n   590\t\n   591\t* The :tfilter:`floatformat` template filter no longer depends on the\n   592\t  ``USE_L10N`` setting and always returns localized output. Use the ``u``\n   593\t  suffix to disable localization.\n   594\t\n   595\t* The default value of the ``USE_L10N`` setting is changed to ``True``. See the\n   596\t  :ref:`Localization section <use_l10n_deprecation>` above for more details.\n   597\t\n   598\t.. _deprecated-features-4.0:\n   599\t\n   600\tFeatures deprecated in 4.0\n   601\t==========================\n   602\t\n   603\tTime zone support\n   604\t-----------------\n   605\t\n   606\tIn order to follow good practice, the default value of the :setting:`USE_TZ`\n   607\tsetting will change from ``False`` to ``True``, and time zone support will be\n   608\tenabled by default, in Django 5.0.\n   609\t\n   610\tNote that the default :file:`settings.py` file created by\n   611\t:djadmin:`django-admin startproject <startproject>` includes\n   612\t:setting:`USE_TZ = True <USE_TZ>` since Django 1.4.\n   613\t\n   614\tYou can set ``USE_TZ`` to ``False`` in your project settings before then to\n   615\topt-out.\n   616\t\n   617\t.. _use_l10n_deprecation:\n   618\t\n   619\tLocalization\n   620\t------------\n   621\t\n   622\tIn order to follow good practice, the default value of the ``USE_L10N`` setting\n   623\tis changed from ``False`` to ``True``.\n   624\t\n   625\tMoreover ``USE_L10N`` is deprecated as of this release. Starting with Django\n   626\t5.0, by default, any date or number displayed by Django will be localized.\n   627\t\n   628\tThe :ttag:`{% localize %} <localize>` tag and the :tfilter:`localize`/\n   629\t:tfilter:`unlocalize` filters will still be honored by Django.\n   630\t\n   631\tMiscellaneous\n   632\t-------------\n   633\t\n   634\t* ``SERIALIZE`` test setting is deprecated as it can be inferred from the\n   635\t  :attr:`~django.test.TestCase.databases` with the\n   636\t  :ref:`serialized_rollback <test-case-serialized-rollback>` option enabled.\n   637\t\n   638\t* The undocumented ``django.utils.baseconv`` module is deprecated.\n   639\t\n   640\t* The undocumented ``django.utils.datetime_safe`` module is deprecated.\n   641\t\n   642\t* The default sitemap protocol for sitemaps built outside the context of a\n   643\t  request will change from ``'http'`` to ``'https'`` in Django 5.0.\n   644\t\n   645\t* The ``extra_tests`` argument for :meth:`.DiscoverRunner.build_suite` and\n   646\t  :meth:`.DiscoverRunner.run_tests` is deprecated.\n   647\t\n   648\t* The :class:`~django.contrib.postgres.aggregates.ArrayAgg`,\n   649\t  :class:`~django.contrib.postgres.aggregates.JSONBAgg`, and\n   650\t  :class:`~django.contrib.postgres.aggregates.StringAgg` aggregates will return\n   651\t  ``None`` when there are no rows instead of ``[]``, ``[]``, and ``''``\n   652\t  respectively in Django 5.0. If you need the previous behavior, explicitly set\n   653\t  ``default`` to ``Value([])``, ``Value('[]')``, or ``Value('')``.\n   654\t\n   655\tFeatures removed in 4.0\n   656\t=======================\n   657\t\n   658\tThese features have reached the end of their deprecation cycle and are removed\n   659\tin Django 4.0.\n   660\t\n   661\tSee :ref:`deprecated-features-3.0` for details on these changes, including how\n   662\tto remove usage of these features.\n   663\t\n   664\t* ``django.utils.http.urlquote()``, ``urlquote_plus()``, ``urlunquote()``, and\n   665\t  ``urlunquote_plus()`` are removed.\n   666\t\n   667\t* ``django.utils.encoding.force_text()`` and ``smart_text()`` are removed.\n   668\t\n   669\t*
