using the model name when\n   404\t  ``Meta.default_related_name`` is set will be removed.\n   405\t\n   406\t* The ``__search`` query lookup and the\n   407\t  ``DatabaseOperations.fulltext_search_sql()`` method will be removed.\n   408\t\n   409\t* The shim for supporting custom related manager classes without a\n   410\t  ``_apply_rel_filters()`` method will be removed.\n   411\t\n   412\t* Using ``User.is_authenticated()`` and ``User.is_anonymous()`` as methods\n   413\t  will no longer be supported.\n   414\t\n   415\t* The private attribute ``virtual_fields`` of ``Model._meta`` will be removed.\n   416\t\n   417\t* The private keyword arguments ``virtual_only`` in\n   418\t  ``Field.contribute_to_class()`` and ``virtual`` in\n   419\t  ``Model._meta.add_field()`` will be removed.\n   420\t\n   421\t* The ``javascript_catalog()`` and ``json_catalog()`` views will be removed.\n   422\t\n   423\t* The ``django.contrib.gis.utils.precision_wkt()`` function will be removed.\n   424\t\n   425\t* In multi-table inheritance, implicit promotion of a ``OneToOneField`` to a\n   426\t  ``parent_link`` will be removed.\n   427\t\n   428\t* Support for ``Widget._format_value()`` will be removed.\n   429\t\n   430\t* ``FileField`` methods ``get_directory_name()`` and ``get_filename()`` will be\n   431\t  removed.\n   432\t\n   433\t* The ``mark_for_escaping()`` function and the classes it uses: ``EscapeData``,\n   434\t  ``EscapeBytes``, ``EscapeText``, ``EscapeString``, and ``EscapeUnicode`` will\n   435\t  be removed.\n   436\t\n   437\t* The ``escape`` filter will change to use\n   438\t  ``django.utils.html.conditional_escape()``.\n   439\t\n   440\t* ``Manager.use_for_related_fields`` will be removed.\n   441\t\n   442\t* Model ``Manager`` inheritance will follow MRO inheritance rules and the\n   443\t  ``Meta.manager_inheritance_from_future`` to opt-in to this behavior will be\n   444\t  removed.\n   445\t\n   446\t* Support for old-style middleware using ``settings.MIDDLEWARE_CLASSES`` will\n   447\t  be removed.\n   448\t\n   449\t.. _deprecation-removed-in-1.10:\n   450\t\n   451\t1.10\n   452\t----\n   453\t\n   454\tSee the :ref:`Django 1.8 release notes<deprecated-features-1.8>` for more\n   455\tdetails on these changes.\n   456\t\n   457\t* Support for calling a ``SQLCompiler`` directly as an alias for calling its\n   458\t  ``quote_name_unless_alias`` method will be removed.\n   459\t\n   460\t* ``cycle`` and ``firstof`` template tags will be removed from the ``future``\n   461\t  template tag library (used during the 1.6/1.7 deprecation period).\n   462\t\n   463\t* ``django.conf.urls.patterns()`` will be removed.\n   464\t\n   465\t* Support for the ``prefix`` argument to\n   466\t  ``django.conf.urls.i18n.i18n_patterns()`` will be removed.\n   467\t\n   468\t* ``SimpleTestCase.urls`` will be removed.\n   469\t\n   470\t* Using an incorrect count of unpacked values in the ``for`` template tag\n   471\t  will raise an exception rather than fail silently.\n   472\t\n   473\t* The ability to reverse URLs using a dotted Python path will be removed.\n   474\t\n   475\t* The ability to use a dotted Python path for the ``LOGIN_URL`` and\n   476\t  ``LOGIN_REDIRECT_URL`` settings will be removed.\n   477\t\n   478\t* Support for :py:mod:`optparse` will be dropped for custom management commands\n   479\t  (replaced by :py:mod:`argparse`).\n   480\t\n   481\t* The class ``django.core.management.NoArgsCommand`` will be removed. Use\n   482\t  :class:`~django.core.management.BaseCommand` instead, which takes no arguments\n   483\t  by default.\n   484\t\n   485\t* ``django.core.context_processors`` module will be removed.\n   486\t\n   487\t* ``django.db.models.sql.aggregates`` module will be removed.\n   488\t\n   489\t* ``django.contrib.gis.db.models.sql.aggregates`` module will be removed.\n   490\t\n   491\t* The following methods and properties of ``django.db.sql.query.Query`` will\n   492\t  be removed:\n   493\t\n   494\t  * Properties: ``aggregates`` and ``aggregate_select``\n   495\t  * Methods: ``add_aggregate``, ``set_aggregate_mask``, and\n   496\t    ``append_aggregate_mask``.\n   497\t\n   498\t* ``django.template.resolve_variable`` will be removed.\n   499\t\n   500\t* The following private APIs will be removed from\n   501\t  :class:`django.db.models.options.Options` (``Model._meta``):\n   502\t\n   503\t  * ``get_field_by_name()``\n   504\t  * ``get_all_field_names()``\n   505\t  * ``get_fields_with_model()``\n   506\t  * ``get_concrete_fields_with_model()``\n   507\t  * ``get_m2m_with_model()``\n   508\t  * ``get_all_related_objects()``\n   509\t  * ``get_all_related_objects_with_model()``\n   510\t  * ``get_all_related_many_to_many_objects()``\n   511\t  * ``get_all_related_m2m_objects_with_model()``\n   512\t\n   513\t* The ``error_message`` argument of ``django.forms.RegexField`` will be removed.\n   514\t\n   515\t* The ``unordered_list`` filter will no longer support old style lists.\n   516\t\n   517\t* Support for string ``view`` arguments to ``url()`` will be removed.\n   518\t\n   519\t* The backward compatible shim  to rename ``django.forms.Form._has_changed()``\n   520\t  to ``has_changed()`` will be removed.\n   521\t\n   522\t* The ``removetags`` template filter will be removed.\n   523\t\n   524\t* The ``remove_tags()`` and ``strip_entities()`` functions in\n   525\t  ``django.utils.html`` will be removed.\n   526\t\n   527\t* The ``is_admin_site`` argument to\n   528\t  ``django.contrib.auth.views.password_reset()`` will be removed.\n   529\t\n   530\t* ``django.db.models.field.subclassing.SubfieldBase`` will be removed.\n   531\t\n   532\t* ``django.utils.checksums`` will be removed; its functionality is included\n   533\t  in ``django-localflavor`` 1.1+.\n   534\t\n   535\t* The ``original_content_type_id`` attribute on\n   536\t  ``django.contrib.admin.helpers.InlineAdminForm`` will be removed.\n   537\t\n   538\t* The backwards compatibility shim to allow ``FormMixin.get_form()`` to be\n   539\t  defined with no default value for its ``form_class`` argument will be removed.\n   540\t\n   541\t* The following settings will be removed:\n   542\t\n   543\t  * ``ALLOWED_INCLUDE_ROOTS``\n   544\t  * ``TEMPLATE_CONTEXT_PROCESSORS``\n   545\t  * ``TEMPLATE_DEBUG``\n   546\t  * ``TEMPLATE_DIRS``\n   547\t  * ``TEMPLATE_LOADERS``\n   548\t  * ``TEMPLATE_STRING_IF_INVALID``\n   549\t\n   550\t* The backwards compatibility alias ``django.template.loader.BaseLoader`` will\n   551\t  be removed.\n   552\t\n   553\t* Django template objects returned by\n   554\t  :func:`~django.template.loader.get_template` and\n   555\t  :func:`~django.template.loader.select_template` won't accept a\n   556\t  :class:`~django.template.Context` in their\n   557\t  :meth:`~django.template.backends.base.Template.render()` method anymore.\n   558\t\n   559\t* :doc:`Template response APIs </ref/template-response>` will enforce the use\n   560\t  of :class:`dict` and backend-dependent template objects instead of\n   561\t  :class:`~django.template.Context` and :class:`~django.template.Template`\n   562\t  respectively.\n   563\t\n   564\t* The ``current_app`` parameter for the following function and classes will be\n   565\t  removed:\n   566\t\n   567\t  * ``django.shortcuts.render()``\n   568\t  * ``django.template.Context()``\n   569\t  * ``django.template.RequestContext()``\n   570\t  * ``django.template.response.TemplateResponse()``\n   571\t\n   572\t* The ``dictionary`` and ``context_instance`` parameters for the following\n   573\t  functions will be removed:\n   574\t\n   575\t  * ``django.shortcuts.render()``\n   576\t  * ``django.shortcuts.render_to_response()``\n   577\t  * ``django.template.loader.render_to_string()``\n   578\t\n   579\t* The ``dirs`` parameter for the following functions will be removed:\n   580\t\n   581\t  * ``django.template.loader.get_template()``\n   582\t  * ``django.template.loader.select_template()``\n   583\t  * ``django.shortcuts.render()``\n   584\t  * ``django.shortcuts.render_to_response()``\n   585\t\n   586\t* Session verification will be enabled regardless of whether or not\n   587\t  ``'django.contrib.auth.middleware.SessionAuthenticationMiddleware'`` is in\n   588\t  ``MIDDLEWARE_CLASSES``.\n   589\t\n   590\t* Private attribute ``django.db.models.Field.related`` will be removed.\n   591\t\n   592\t* The ``--list`` option of the ``migrate`` management command will be removed.\n   593\t\n   594\t* The ``ssi`` template tag will be removed.\n   595\t\n   596\t* Support for the ``=`` comparison operator in the ``if`` template tag will be\n   597\t  removed.\n   598\t\n   599\t* The backwards compatibility shims to allow ``Storage.get_available_name()``\n   600\t  and ``Storage.save()`` to be defined without a ``max_length`` argument will\n   601\t  be removed.\n   602\t\n   603\t* Support for the legacy ``%(<foo>)s`` syntax in ``ModelFormMixin.success_url``\n   604\t  will be removed.\n   605\t\n   606\t* ``GeoQuerySet`` aggregate methods ``collect()``, ``extent()``, ``extent3d()``,\n   607\t  ``make_line()``, and ``unionagg()`` will be removed.\n   608\t\n   609\t* Ability to specify ``ContentType.name`` when creating a content type instance\n   610\t  will be removed.\n   611\t\n   612\t* Support for the old signature of ``allow_migrate`` will be removed. It changed\n   613\t  from ``allow_migrate(self, db, model)`` to\n   614\t  ``allow_migrate(self, db, app_label, model_name=None, **hints)``.\n   615\t\n   616\t* Support for the syntax of ``{% cycle %}`` that uses comma-separated arguments\n   617\t  will be removed.\n   618\t\n   619\t* The warning that :class:`~django.core.signing.Signer` issues when given an\n   620\t  invalid separator will become an exception.\n   621\t\n   622\t.. _deprecation-removed-in-1.9:\n   623\t\n   624\t1.9\n   625\t---\n   626\t\n   627\tSee the :ref:`Django 1.7 release notes<deprecated-features-1.7>` for more\n   628\tdetails on these changes.\n   629\t\n   630\t* ``django.utils.dictconfig`` will be removed.\n   631\t\n   632\t* ``django.utils.importlib`` will be removed.\n   633\t\n   634\t* ``django.utils.tzinfo`` will be removed.\n   635\t\n   636\t* ``django.utils.unittest`` will be removed.\n   637\t\n   638\t* The ``syncdb`` command will be removed.\n   639\t\n   640\t* ``django.db.models.signals.pre_syncdb`` and\n   641\t  ``django.db.models.signals.post_syncdb`` will be removed.\n   642\t\n   643\t* ``allow_syncdb`` on database routers will no longer automatically become\n   644\t  ``allow_migrate``.\n   645\t\n   646\t* Automatic syncing of apps without migrations will be removed. Migrations will\n   647\t  become compulsory for all apps unless you pass the ``--run-syncdb`` option to\n   648\t  ``migrate``.\n   649\t\n   650\t* The SQL management commands for apps without migrations, ``sql``, ``sqlall``,\n   651\t  ``sqlclear``, ``sqldropindexes``, and ``sqlindexes``, will be removed.\n   652\t\n   653\t* Support for automatic loading of ``initial_data`` fixtures and initial SQL\n   654\t  data will be removed.\n   655\t\n   656\t* All models will need to be defined inside an installed application or\n   657\t  declare an explicit :attr:`~django.db.models.Options.app_label`.\n   658\t  Furthermore, it won't be possible to import them before their application\n   659\t  is loaded. In particular, it won't be possible to import models inside\n   660\t  the root package of their application.\n   661\t\n   662\t* The model and form ``IPAddressField`` will be removed. A stub field will\n   663\t  remain for compatibility with historical migrations.\n   664\t\n   665\t* ``AppCommand.handle_app()`` will no longer be supported.\n   666\t\n   667\t* ``RequestSite`` and ``get_current_site()`` will no longer be importable from\n   668\t  ``django.contrib.sites.models``.\n   669\t\n   670\t* FastCGI support via the ``runfcgi`` management command will be\n   671\t  removed. Please deploy your project using WSGI.\n   672\t\n   673\t* ``django.utils.datastructures.SortedDict`` will be removed. Use\n   674\t  :class:`collections.OrderedDict` from the Python standard library instead.\n   675\t\n   676\t* ``ModelAdmin.declared_fieldsets`` will be removed.\n   677\t\n   678\t* Instances of ``util.py`` in the Django codebase have been renamed to\n   679\t  ``utils.py`` in an effort to unify all util and utils references.\n   680\t  The modules that provided backwards compatibility will be removed:\n   681\t\n   682\t  * ``django.contrib.admin.util``\n   683\t  * ``django.contrib.gis.db.backends.util``\n   684\t  * ``django.db.backends.util``\n   685\t  * ``django.forms.util``\n   686\t\n   687\t* ``ModelAdmin.get_formsets`` will be removed.\n   688\t\n   689\t* The backward compatibility shim introduced to rename the\n   690\t  ``BaseMemcachedCache._get_memcache_timeout()`` method to\n   691\t  ``get_backend_timeout()`` will be removed.\n   692\t\n   693\t* The ``--natural`` and ``-n`` options for :djadmin:`dumpdata` will be removed.\n   694\t\n   695\t* The ``use_natural_keys`` argument for ``serializers.serialize()`` will be\n   696\t  removed.\n   697\t\n   698\t* Private API ``django.forms.forms.get_declared_fields()`` will be removed.\n   699\t\n   700\t* The ability to use a ``SplitDateTimeWidget`` with ``DateTimeField`` will be\n   701\t  removed.\n   702\t\n   703\t* The ``WSGIRequest.REQUEST`` property will be removed.\n   704\t\n   705\t* The class ``django.utils.datastructures.MergeDict`` will be removed.\n   706\t\n   707\t* The ``zh-cn`` and ``zh-tw`` language codes will be removed and have been\n   708\t  replaced by the ``zh-hans`` and ``zh-hant`` language code respectively.\n   709\t\n   710\t* The internal ``django.utils.functional.memoize`` will be removed.\n   711\t\n   712\t* ``django.core.cache.get_cache`` will be removed. Add suitable entries\n   713\t  to :setting:`CACHES` and use :data:`django.core.cache.caches` instead.\n   714\t\n   715\t* ``django.db.models.loading`` will be removed.\n   716\t\n   717\t* Passing callable arguments to querysets will no longer be possible.\n   718\t\n   719\t* ``BaseCommand.requires_model_validation`` will be removed in favor of\n   720\t  ``requires_system_checks``. Admin validators will be replaced by admin\n   721\t  checks.\n   722\t\n   723\t* The ``ModelAdmin.validator_class`` and ``default_validator_class`` attributes\n   724\t  will be removed.\n   725\t\n   726\t* ``ModelAdmin.validate()`` will be removed.\n   727\t\n   728\t* ``django.db.backends.DatabaseValidation.validate_field`` will be removed in\n   729\t  favor of the ``check_field`` method.\n   730\t\n   731\t* The ``validate`` management command will be removed.\n   732\t\n   733\t* ``django.utils.module_loading.import_by_path`` will be removed in favor of\n   734\t  ``django.utils.module_loading.import_string``.\n   735\t\n   736\t* ``ssi`` and ``url`` template tags will be removed from the ``future`` template\n   737\t  tag library (used during the 1.3/1.4 deprecation period).\n   738\t\n   739\t* ``django.utils.text.javascript_quote`` will be removed.\n   740\t\n   741\t* Database test settings as independent entries in the database settings,\n   742\t  prefixed by ``TEST_``, will no longer be supported.\n   743\t\n   744\t* The ``cache_choices`` option to :class:`~django.forms.ModelChoiceField` and\n   745\t  :class:`~django.forms.ModelMultipleChoiceField` will be removed.\n   746\t\n   747\t* The default value of the\n   748\t  :attr:`RedirectView.permanent <django.views.generic.base.RedirectView.permanent>`\n   749\t  attribute will change from ``True`` to ``False``.\n   750\t\n   751\t* ``django.contrib.sitemaps.FlatPageSitemap`` will be removed in favor of\n   752\t  ``django.contrib.flatpages.sitemaps.FlatPageSitemap``.\n   753\t\n   754\t* Private API ``django.test.utils.TestTemplateLoader`` will be removed.\n   755\t\n   756\t* The ``django.contrib.contenttypes.generic`` module will be removed.\n   757\t\n   758\t* Private APIs ``django.db.models.sql.where.WhereNode.make_atom()`` and\n   759\t  ``django.db.models.sql.where.Constraint`` will be removed.\n   760\t\n   761\t.. _deprecation-removed-in-1.8:\n   762\t\n   763\t1.8\n   764\t---\n   765\t\n   766\tSee the :ref:`Django 1.6 release notes<deprecated-features-1.6>` for more\n   767\tdetails on these changes.\n   768\t\n   769\t* ``django.contrib.comments`` will be removed.\n   770\t\n   771\t* The following transaction management APIs will be removed:\n   772\t\n   773\t  - ``TransactionMiddleware``,\n   774\t  - the decorators and context managers ``autocommit``, ``commit_on_success``,\n   775\t    and ``commit_manually``, defined in ``django.db.transaction``,\n   776\t  - the functions ``commit_unless_managed`` and ``rollback_unless_managed``,\n   777\t    also defined in ``django.db.transaction``,\n   778\t  - the ``TRANSACTIONS_MANAGED`` setting.\n   779\t\n   780\t* The :ttag:`cycle` and :ttag:`firstof` template tags will auto-escape their\n   781\t  arguments. In 1.6 and 1.7, this behavior is provided by the version of these\n   782\t  tags in the ``future`` template tag library.\n   783\t\n   784\t* The ``SEND_BROKEN_LINK_EMAILS`` setting will be removed. Add the\n   785\t  :class:`django.middleware.common.BrokenLinkEmailsMiddleware` middleware to\n   786\t  your ``MIDDLEWARE_CLASSES`` setting instead.\n   787\t\n   788\t* ``django.middleware.doc.XViewMiddleware`` will be removed. Use\n   789\t  ``django.contrib.admindocs.middleware.XViewMiddleware`` instead.\n   790\t\n   791\t* ``Model._meta.module_name`` was renamed to ``model_name``.\n   792\t\n   793\t* Remove the backward compatible shims introduced to rename ``get_query_set``\n   794\t  and similar queryset methods. This affects the following classes:\n   795\t  ``BaseModelAdmin``, ``ChangeList``, ``BaseCommentNode``,\n   796\t  ``GenericForeignKey``, ``Manager``, ``SingleRelatedObjectDescriptor`` and\n   797\t  ``ReverseSingleRelatedObjectDescriptor``.\n   798\t\n   799\t* Remove the backward compatible shims introduced to rename the attributes\n   800\t  ``ChangeList.root_query_set`` and ``ChangeList.query_set``.\n   801\t\n   802\t* ``django.views.defaults.shortcut`` will be removed, as part of the\n   803\t  goal of removing all ``django.contrib`` references from the core\n   804\t  Django codebase. Instead use\n   805\t  ``django.contrib.contenttypes.views.shortcut``. ``django.conf.urls.shortcut``\n   806\t  will also be removed.\n   807\t\n   808\t* Support for the Python Imaging Library (PIL) module will be removed, as it\n   809\t  no longer appears to be actively maintained & does not work on Python 3.\n   810\t\n   811\t* The following private APIs will be removed:\n   812\t\n   813\t  - ``django.db.backend``\n   814\t  - ``django.db.close_connection()``\n   815\t  - ``django.db.backends.creation.BaseDatabaseCreation.set_autocommit()``\n   816\t  - ``django.db.transaction.is_managed()``\n   817\t  - ``django.db.transaction.managed()``\n   818\t\n   819\t* ``django.forms.widgets.RadioInput`` will be removed in favor of\n   820\t  ``django.forms.widgets.RadioChoiceInput``.\n   821\t\n   822\t* The module ``django.test.simple`` and the class\n   823\t  ``django.test.simple.DjangoTestSuiteRunner`` will be removed. Instead use\n   824\t  ``django.test.runner.DiscoverRunner``.\n   825\t\n   826\t* The module ``django.test._doctest`` will be removed. Instead use the doctest\n   827\t  module from the Python standard library.\n   828\t\n   829\t* The ``CACHE_MIDDLEWARE_ANONYMOUS_ONLY`` setting will be removed.\n   830\t\n   831\t* Usage of the hard-coded *Hold down \"Control\", or \"Command\" on a Mac, to select\n   832\t  more than one.* string to override or append to user-provided ``help_text`` in\n   833\t  forms for ManyToMany model fields will not be performed by Django anymore\n   834\t  either at the model or forms layer.\n   835\t\n   836\t* The ``Model._meta.get_(add|change|delete)_permission`` methods will\n   837\t  be removed.\n   838\t\n   839\t* The session key ``django_language`` will no longer be read for backwards\n   840\t  compatibility.\n   841\t\n   842\t* Geographic Sitemaps will be removed\n   843\t  (``django.contrib.gis.sitemaps.views.index`` and\n   844\t  ``django.contrib.gis.sitemaps.views.sitemap``).\n   845\t\n   846\t* ``django.utils.html.fix_ampersands``, the ``fix_ampersands`` template filter and\n   847\t  ``django.utils.html.clean_html`` will be removed following an accelerated deprecation.\n   848\t\n   849\t.. _deprecation-removed-in-1.7:\n   850\t\n   851\t1.7\n   852\t---\n   853\t\n   854\tSee the :ref:`Django 1.5 release notes<deprecated-features-1.5>` for more\n   855\tdetails on these changes.\n   856\t\n   857\t* The module ``django.utils.simplejson`` will be removed. The standard library\n   858\t  provides :mod:`json` which should be used instead.\n   859\t\n   860\t* The function ``django.utils.itercompat.product`` will be removed. The Python\n   861\t  builtin version should be used instead.\n   862\t\n   863\t* Auto-correction of INSTALLED_APPS and TEMPLATE_DIRS settings when they are\n   864\t  specified as a plain string instead of a tuple will be removed and raise an\n   865\t  exception.\n   866\t\n   867\t* The ``mimetype`` argument to the ``__init__`` methods of\n   868\t  :class:`~django.http.HttpResponse`,\n   869\t  :class:`~django.template.response.SimpleTemplateResponse`, and\n   870\t  :class:`~django.template.response.TemplateResponse`, will be removed.\n   871\t  ``content_type`` should be used instead. This also applies to the\n   872\t  ``render_to_response()`` shortcut and the sitemap views,\n   873\t  :func:`~django.contrib.sitemaps.views.index` and\n   874\t  :func:`~django.contrib.sitemaps.views.sitemap`.\n   875\t\n   876\t* When :class:`~django.http.HttpResponse` is instantiated with an iterator,\n   877\t  or when :attr:`~django.http.HttpResponse.content` is set to an iterator,\n   878\t  that iterator will be immediately consumed.\n   879\t\n   880\t* The ``AUTH_PROFILE_MODULE`` setting, and the ``get_profile()`` method on\n   881\t  the User model, will be removed.\n   882\t\n   883\t* The ``cleanup`` management command will be removed. It's replaced by\n   884\t  ``clearsessions``.\n   885\t\n   886\t* The ``daily_cleanup.py`` script will be removed.\n   887\t\n   888\t* The ``depth`` keyword argument will be removed from\n   889\t  :meth:`~django.db.models.query.QuerySet.select_related`.\n   890\t\n   891\t* The undocumented ``get_warnings_state()``/``restore_warnings_state()``\n   892\t  functions from :mod:`django.test.utils` and the ``save_warnings_state()``/\n   893\t  ``restore_warnings_state()``\n   894\t  :ref:`django.test.*TestCase <django-testcase-subclasses>` methods are\n   895\t  deprecated. Use the :class:`warnings.catch_warnings` context manager\n   896\t  available starting with Python 2.6 instead.\n   897\t\n   898\t* The undocumented ``check_for_test_cookie`` method in\n   899\t  :class:`~django.contrib.auth.forms.AuthenticationForm` will be removed\n   900\t  following an accelerated deprecation. Users subclassing this form should\n   901\t  remove calls to this method, and instead ensure that their auth related views\n   902\t  are CSRF protected, which ensures that cookies are enabled.\n   903\t\n   904\t* The version of ``django.contrib.auth.views.password_reset_confirm()`` that\n   905\t  supports base36 encoded user IDs\n   906\t  (``django.contrib.auth.views.password_reset_confirm_uidb36``) will be\n   907\t  removed. If your site has been running Django 1.6 for more than\n   908\t  ``PASSWORD_RESET_TIMEOUT_DAYS``, this change will have no effect. If not,\n   909\t  then any password reset links generated before you upgrade to Django 1.7\n   910\t  won't work after the upgrade.\n   911\t\n   912\t* The ``django.utils.encoding.StrAndUnicode`` mix-in will be removed.\n   913\t\n   914\t.. _deprecation-removed-in-1.6:\n   915\t\n   916\t1.6\n   917\t---\n   918\t\n   919\tSee the :ref:`Django 1.4 release notes<deprecated-features-1.4>` for more\n   920\tdetails on these changes.\n   921\t\n   922\t* ``django.contrib.databrowse`` will be removed.\n   923\t\n   924\t* ``django.contrib.localflavor`` will be removed following an accelerated\n   925\t  deprecation.\n   926\t\n   927\t* ``django.contrib.markup`` will be removed following an accelerated\n   928\t  deprecation.\n   929\t\n   930\t* The compatibility modules ``django.utils.copycompat`` and\n   931\t  ``django.utils.hashcompat`` as well as the functions\n   932\t  ``django.utils.itercompat.all`` and ``django.utils.itercompat.any`` will\n   933\t  be removed. The Python builtin versions should be used instead.\n   934\t\n   935\t* The ``csrf_response_exempt`` and ``csrf_view_exempt`` decorators will\n   936\t  be removed. Since 1.4 ``csrf_response_exempt`` has been a no-op (it\n   937\t  returns the same function), and ``csrf_view_exempt`` has been a\n   938\t  synonym for ``django.views.decorators.csrf.csrf_exempt``, which should\n   939\t  be used to replace it.\n   940\t\n   941\t* The ``django.core.cache.backends.memcached.CacheClass`` backend\n   942\t  was split into two in Django 1.3 in order to introduce support for\n   943\t  PyLibMC. The historical ``CacheClass`` will be removed in favor of\n   944\t  ``django.core.cache.backends.memcached.MemcachedCache``.\n   945\t\n   946\t* The UK-prefixed objects of ``django.contrib.localflavor.uk`` will only\n   947\t  be accessible through their GB-prefixed names (GB is the correct\n   948\t  ISO 3166 code for United Kingdom).\n   949\t\n   950\t* The ``IGNORABLE_404_STARTS`` and ``IGNORABLE_404_ENDS`` settings have been\n   951\t  superseded by :setting:`IGNORABLE_404_URLS` in the 1.4 release. They will be\n   952\t  removed.\n   953\t\n   954\t* The form wizard has been refactored to use class-based views with pluggable\n   955\t  backends in 1.4. The previous implementation will be removed.\n   956\t\n   957\t* Legacy ways of calling\n   958\t  :func:`~django.views.decorators.cache.cache_page` will be removed.\n   959\t\n   960\t* The backward-compatibility shim to automatically add a debug-false\n   961\t  filter to the ``'mail_admins'`` logging handler will be removed. The\n   962\t  :setting:`LOGGING` setting should include this filter explicitly if\n   963\t  it is desired.\n   964\t\n   965\t* The builtin truncation functions ``django.utils.text.truncate_words()``\n   966\t  and ``django.utils.text.truncate_html_words()`` will be removed in\n   967\t  favor of the ``django.utils.text.Truncator`` class.\n   968\t\n   969\t* The ``django.contrib.gis.geoip.GeoIP`` class was moved to\n   970\t  ``django.contrib.gis.geoip`` in 1.4 -- the shortcut in\n   971\t  ``django.contrib.gis.utils`` will be removed.\n   972\t\n   973\t* ``django.conf.urls.defaults`` will be removed. The functions\n   974\t  ``include()``, ``patterns()``, and ``url()``, plus\n   975\t  :data:`~django.conf.urls.handler404` and :data:`~django.conf.urls.handler500`\n   976\t  are now available through ``django.conf.urls``.\n   977\t\n   978\t* The functions ``setup_environ()`` and ``execute_manager()`` will be removed\n   979\t  from :mod:`django.core.management`. This also means that the old (pre-1.4)\n   980\t  style of :file:`manage.py` file will no longer work.\n   981\t\n   982\t* Setting the ``is_safe`` and ``needs_autoescape`` flags as attributes of\n   983\t  template filter functions will no longer be supported.\n   984\t\n   985\t* The attribute ``HttpRequest.raw_post_data`` was renamed to ``HttpRequest.body``\n   986\t  in 1.4. The backward compatibility will be removed --\n   987\t  ``HttpRequest.raw_post_data`` will no longer work.\n   988\t\n   989\t* The value for the ``post_url_continue`` parameter in\n   990\t  ``ModelAdmin.response_add()`` will have to be either ``None`` (to redirect\n   991\t  to the newly created object's edit page) or a pre-formatted url. For introductory material, see the :doc:`settings topic\n    22\tguide </topics/settings>`.\n    23\t\n    24\t.. setting:: ABSOLUTE_URL_OVERRIDES\n    25\t\n    26\t``ABSOLUTE_URL_OVERRIDES``\n    27\t--------------------------\n    28\t\n    29\tDefault: ``{}`` (Empty dictionary)\n    30\t\n    31\tA dictionary mapping ``\"app_label.model_name\"`` strings to functions that take\n    32\ta model object and return its URL. This is a way of inserting or overriding\n    33\t``get_absolute_url()`` methods on a per-installation basis. Example::\n    34\t\n    35\t    ABSOLUTE_URL_OVERRIDES = {\n    36\t        'blogs.blog': lambda o: \"/blogs/%s/\" % o.slug,\n    37\t        'news.story': lambda o: \"/stories/%s/%s/\" % (o.pub_year, o.slug),\n    38\t    }\n    39\t\n    40\tThe model name used in this setting should be all lowercase, regardless of the\n    41\tcase of the actual model class name.\n    42\t\n    43\t.. setting:: ADMINS\n    44\t\n    45\t``ADMINS``\n    46\t----------\n    47\t\n    48\tDefault: ``[]`` (Empty list)\n    49\t\n    50\tA list of all the people who get code error notifications. When\n    51\t:setting:`DEBUG=False <DEBUG>` and :class:`~django.utils.log.AdminEmailHandler`\n    52\tis configured in :setting:`LOGGING` (done by default), Django emails these\n    53\tpeople the details of exceptions raised in the request/response cycle.\n    54\t\n    55\tEach item in the list should be a tuple of (Full name, email address). Example::\n    56\t\n    57\t    [('John', 'john@example.com'), ('Mary', 'mary@example.com')]\n    58\t\n    59\t.. setting:: ALLOWED_HOSTS\n    60\t\n    61\t``ALLOWED_HOSTS``\n    62\t-----------------\n    63\t\n    64\tDefault: ``[]`` (Empty list)\n    65\t\n    66\tA list of strings representing the host/domain names that this Django site can\n    67\tserve. This is a security measure to prevent :ref:`HTTP Host header attacks\n    68\t<host-headers-virtual-hosting>`, which are possible even under many\n    69\tseemingly-safe web server configurations.\n    70\t\n    71\tValues in this list can be fully qualified names (e.g. ``'www.example.com'``),\n    72\tin which case they will be matched against the request's ``Host`` header\n    73\texactly (case-insensitive, not including port). A value beginning with a period\n    74\tcan be used as a subdomain wildcard: ``'.example.com'`` will match\n    75\t``example.com``, ``www.example.com``, and any other subdomain of\n    76\t``example.com``. A value of ``'*'`` will match anything; in this case you are\n    77\tresponsible to provide your own validation of the ``Host`` header (perhaps in a\n    78\tmiddleware; if so this middleware must be listed first in\n    79\t:setting:`MIDDLEWARE`).\n    80\t\n    81\tDjango also allows the `fully qualified domain name (FQDN)`_ of any entries.\n    82\tSome browsers include a trailing dot in the ``Host`` header which Django\n    83\tstrips when performing host validation.\n    84\t\n    85\t.. _`fully qualified domain name (FQDN)`: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fully_qualified_domain_name\n    86\t\n    87\tIf the ``Host`` header (or ``X-Forwarded-Host`` if\n    88\t:setting:`USE_X_FORWARDED_HOST` is enabled) does not match any value in this\n    89\tlist, the :meth:`django.http.HttpRequest.get_host()` method will raise\n    90\t:exc:`~django.core.exceptions.SuspiciousOperation`.\n    91\t\n    92\tWhen :setting:`DEBUG` is ``True`` and ``ALLOWED_HOSTS`` is empty, the host\n    93\tis validated against ``['.localhost', '127.0.0.1', '[::1]']``.\n    94\t\n    95\t``ALLOWED_HOSTS`` is also :ref:`checked when running tests\n    96\t<topics-testing-advanced-multiple-hosts>`.\n    97\t\n    98\tThis validation only applies via :meth:`~django.http.HttpRequest.get_host()`;\n    99\tif your code accesses the ``Host`` header directly from ``request.META`` you\n   100\tare bypassing this security protection.\n   101\t\n   102\t.. setting:: APPEND_SLASH\n   103\t\n   104\t``APPEND_SLASH``\n   105\t----------------\n   106\t\n   107\tDefault: ``True``\n   108\t\n   109\tWhen set to ``True``, if the request URL does not match any of the patterns\n   110\tin the URLconf and it doesn't end in a slash, an HTTP redirect is issued to the\n   111\tsame URL with a slash appended. Note that the redirect may cause any data\n   112\tsubmitted in a POST request to be lost.\n   113\t\n   114\tThe :setting:`APPEND_SLASH` setting is only used if\n   115\t:class:`~django.middleware.common.CommonMiddleware` is installed\n   116\t(see :doc:`/topics/http/middleware`). See also :setting:`PREPEND_WWW`.\n   117\t\n   118\t.. setting:: CACHES\n   119\t\n   120\t``CACHES``\n   121\t----------\n   122\t\n   123\tDefault::\n   124\t\n   125\t    {\n   126\t        'default': {\n   127\t            'BACKEND': 'django.core.cache.backends.locmem.LocMemCache',\n   128\t        }\n   129\t    }\n   130\t\n   131\tA dictionary containing the settings for all caches to be used with\n   132\tDjango. It is a nested dictionary whose contents maps cache aliases\n   133\tto a dictionary containing the options for an individual cache.\n   134\t\n   135\tThe :setting:`CACHES` setting must configure a ``default`` cache;\n   136\tany number of additional caches may also be specified. If you\n   137\tare using a cache backend other than the local memory cache, or\n   138\tyou need to define multiple caches, other options will be required.\n   139\tThe following cache options are available.\n   140\t\n   141\t.. setting:: CACHES-BACKEND\n   142\t\n   143\t``BACKEND``\n   144\t~~~~~~~~~~~\n   145\t\n   146\tDefault: ``''`` (Empty string)\n   147\t\n   148\tThe cache backend to use. The built-in cache backends are:\n   149\t\n   150\t* ``'django.core.cache.backends.db.DatabaseCache'``\n   151\t* ``'django.core.cache.backends.dummy.DummyCache'``\n   152\t* ``'django.core.cache.backends.filebased.FileBasedCache'``\n   153\t* ``'django.core.cache.backends.locmem.LocMemCache'``\n   154\t* ``'django.core.cache.backends.memcached.PyMemcacheCache'``\n   155\t* ``'django.core.cache.backends.memcached.PyLibMCCache'``\n   156\t* ``'django.core.cache.backends.redis.RedisCache'``\n   157\t\n   158\tYou can use a cache backend that doesn't ship with Django by setting\n   159\t:setting:`BACKEND <CACHES-BACKEND>` to a fully-qualified path of a cache\n   160\tbackend class (i.e. ``mypackage.backends.whatever.WhateverCache``).\n   161\t\n   162\t.. versionchanged:: 3.2\n   163\t\n   164\t    The ``PyMemcacheCache`` backend was added.\n   165\t\n   166\t.. versionchanged:: 4.0\n   167\t\n   168\t    The ``RedisCache`` backend was added.\n   169\t\n   170\t.. setting:: CACHES-KEY_FUNCTION\n   171\t\n   172\t``KEY_FUNCTION``\n   173\t~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   174\t\n   175\tA string containing a dotted path to a function (or any callable) that defines how to\n   176\tcompose a prefix, version and key into a final cache key. The default\n   177\timplementation is equivalent to the function::\n   178\t\n   179\t    def make_key(key, key_prefix, version):\n   180\t        return ':'.join([key_prefix, str(version), key])\n   181\t\n   182\tYou may use any key function you want, as long as it has the same\n   183\targument signature.\n   184\t\n   185\tSee the :ref:`cache documentation <cache_key_transformation>` for more\n   186\tinformation.\n   187\t\n   188\t.. setting:: CACHES-KEY_PREFIX\n   189\t\n   190\t``KEY_PREFIX``\n   191\t~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   192\t\n   193\tDefault: ``''`` (Empty string)\n   194\t\n   195\tA string that will be automatically included (prepended by default) to\n   196\tall cache keys used by the Django server.\n   197\t\n   198\tSee the :ref:`cache documentation <cache_key_prefixing>` for more information.\n   199\t\n   200\t.. setting:: CACHES-LOCATION\n   201\t\n   202\t``LOCATION``\n   203\t~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   204\t\n   205\tDefault: ``''`` (Empty string)\n   206\t\n   207\tThe location of the cache to use. This might be the directory for a\n   208\tfile system cache, a host and port for a memcache server, or an identifying\n   209\tname for a local memory cache. e.g.::\n   210\t\n   211\t    CACHES = {\n   212\t        'default': {\n   213\t            'BACKEND': 'django.core.cache.backends.filebased.FileBasedCache',\n   214\t            'LOCATION': '/var/tmp/django_cache',\n   215\t        }\n   216\t    }\n   217\t\n   218\t.. setting:: CACHES-OPTIONS\n   219\t\n   220\t``OPTIONS``\n   221\t~~~~~~~~~~~\n   222\t\n   223\tDefault: ``None``\n   224\t\n   225\tExtra parameters to pass to the cache backend. Available parameters\n   226\tvary depending on your cache backend.\n   227\t\n   228\tSome information on available parameters can be found in the\n   229\t:ref:`cache arguments <cache_arguments>` documentation. For more information,\n   230\tconsult your backend module's own documentation.\n   231\t\n   232\t.. setting:: CACHES-TIMEOUT\n   233\t\n   234\t``TIMEOUT``\n   235\t~~~~~~~~~~~\n   236\t\n   237\tDefault: ``300``\n   238\t\n   239\tThe number of seconds before a cache entry is considered stale. If the value of\n   240\tthis setting is ``None``, cache entries will not expire. A value of ``0``\n   241\tcauses keys to immediately expire (effectively \"don't cache\").\n   242\t\n   243\t.. setting:: CACHES-VERSION\n   244\t\n   245\t``VERSION``\n   246\t~~~~~~~~~~~\n   247\t\n   248\tDefault: ``1``\n   249\t\n   250\tThe default version number for cache keys generated by the Django server.\n   251\t\n   252\tSee the :ref:`cache documentation <cache_versioning>` for more information.\n   253\t\n   254\t.. setting:: CACHE_MIDDLEWARE_ALIAS\n   255\t\n   256\t``CACHE_MIDDLEWARE_ALIAS``\n   257\t--------------------------\n   258\t\n   259\tDefault: ``'default'``\n   260\t\n   261\tThe cache connection to use for the :ref:`cache middleware\n   262\t<the-per-site-cache>`.\n   263\t\n   264\t.. setting:: CACHE_MIDDLEWARE_KEY_PREFIX\n   265\t\n   266\t``CACHE_MIDDLEWARE_KEY_PREFIX``\n   267\t-------------------------------\n   268\t\n   269\tDefault: ``''`` (Empty string)\n   270\t\n   271\tA string which will be prefixed to the cache keys generated by the :ref:`cache\n   272\tmiddleware <the-per-site-cache>`. This prefix is combined with the\n   273\t:setting:`KEY_PREFIX <CACHES-KEY_PREFIX>` setting; it does not replace it.\n   274\t\n   275\tSee :doc:`/topics/cache`.\n   276\t\n   277\t.. setting:: CACHE_MIDDLEWARE_SECONDS\n   278\t\n   279\t``CACHE_MIDDLEWARE_SECONDS``\n   280\t----------------------------\n   281\t\n   282\tDefault: ``600``\n   283\t\n   284\tThe default number of seconds to cache a page for the :ref:`cache middleware\n   285\t<the-per-site-cache>`.\n   286\t\n   287\tSee :doc:`/topics/cache`.\n   288\t\n   289\t.. _settings-csrf:\n   290\t\n   291\t.. setting:: CSRF_COOKIE_AGE\n   292\t\n   293\t``CSRF_COOKIE_AGE``\n   294\t-------------------\n   295\t\n   296\tDefault: ``31449600`` (approximately 1 year, in seconds)\n   297\t\n   298\tThe age of CSRF cookies, in seconds.\n   299\t\n   300\tThe reason for setting a long-lived expiration time is to avoid problems in\n   301\tthe case of a user closing a browser or bookmarking a page and then loading\n   302\tthat page from a browser cache. Without persistent cookies, the form submission\n   303\twould fail in this case.\n   304\t\n   305\tSome browsers (specifically Internet Explorer) can disallow the use of\n   306\tpersistent cookies or can have the indexes to the cookie jar corrupted on disk,\n   307\tthereby causing CSRF protection checks to (sometimes intermittently) fail.\n   308\tChange this setting to ``None`` to use session-based CSRF cookies, which\n   309\tkeep the cookies in-memory instead of on persistent storage.\n   310\t\n   311\t.. setting:: CSRF_COOKIE_DOMAIN\n   312\t\n   313\t``CSRF_COOKIE_DOMAIN``\n   314\t----------------------\n   315\t\n   316\tDefault: ``None``\n   317\t\n   318\tThe domain to be used when setting the CSRF cookie.  This can be useful for\n   319\teasily allowing cross-subdomain requests to be excluded from the normal cross\n   320\tsite request forgery protection.  It should be set to a string such as\n   321\t``\".example.com\"`` to allow a POST request from a form on one subdomain to be\n   322\taccepted by a view served from another subdomain.\n   323\t\n   324\tPlease note that the presence of this setting does not imply that Django's CSRF\n   325\tprotection is safe from cross-subdomain attacks by default - please see the\n   326\t:ref:`CSRF limitations <csrf-limitations>` section.\n   327\t\n   328\t.. setting:: CSRF_COOKIE_HTTPONLY\n   329\t\n   330\t``CSRF_COOKIE_HTTPONLY``\n   331\t------------------------\n   332\t\n   333\tDefault: ``False``\n   334\t\n   335\tWhether to use ``HttpOnly`` flag on the CSRF cookie. If this is set to\n   336\t``True``, client-side JavaScript will not be able to access the CSRF cookie.\n   337\t\n   338\tDesignating the CSRF cookie as ``HttpOnly`` doesn't offer any practical\n   339\tprotection because CSRF is only to protect against cross-domain attacks. If an\n   340\tattacker can read the cookie via JavaScript, they're already on the same domain\n   341\tas far as the browser knows, so they can do anything they like anyway. (XSS is\n   342\ta much bigger hole than CSRF.)\n   343\t\n   344\tAlthough the setting offers little practical benefit, it's sometimes required\n   345\tby security auditors.\n   346\t\n   347\tIf you enable this and need to send the value of the CSRF token with an AJAX\n   348\trequest, your JavaScript must pull the value :ref:`from a hidden CSRF token\n   349\tform input <acquiring-csrf-token-from-html>` instead of :ref:`from the cookie\n   350\t<acquiring-csrf-token-from-cookie>`.\n   351\t\n   352\tSee :setting:`SESSION_COOKIE_HTTPONLY` for details on ``HttpOnly``.\n   353\t\n   354\t.. setting:: CSRF_COOKIE_NAME\n   355\t\n   356\t``CSRF_COOKIE_NAME``\n   357\t--------------------\n   358\t\n   359\tDefault: ``'csrftoken'``\n   360\t\n   361\tThe name of the cookie to use for the CSRF authentication token. This can be\n   362\twhatever you want (as long as it's different from the other cookie names in\n   363\tyour application). See :doc:`/ref/csrf`.\n   364\t\n   365\t.. setting:: CSRF_COOKIE_PATH\n   366\t\n   367\t``CSRF_COOKIE_PATH``\n   368\t--------------------\n   369\t\n   370\tDefault: ``'/'``\n   371\t\n   372\tThe path set on the CSRF cookie. This should either match the URL path of your\n   373\tDjango installation or be a parent of that path.\n   374\t\n   375\tThis is useful if you have multiple Django instances running under the same\n   376\thostname. They can use different cookie paths, and each instance will only see\n   377\tits own CSRF cookie.\n   378\t\n   379\t.. setting:: CSRF_COOKIE_SAMESITE\n   380\t\n   381\t``CSRF_COOKIE_SAMESITE``\n   382\t------------------------\n   383\t\n   384\tDefault: ``'Lax'``\n   385\t\n   386\tThe value of the `SameSite`_ flag on the CSRF cookie. This flag prevents the\n   387\tcookie from being sent in cross-site requests.\n   388\t\n   389\tSee :setting:`SESSION_COOKIE_SAMESITE` for details about ``SameSite``.\n   390\t\n   391\t.. setting:: CSRF_COOKIE_SECURE\n   392\t\n   393\t``CSRF_COOKIE_SECURE``\n   394\t----------------------\n   395\t\n   396\tDefault: ``False``\n   397\t\n   398\tWhether to use a secure cookie for the CSRF cookie. If this is set to ``True``,\n   399\tthe cookie will be marked as \"secure\", which means browsers may ensure that the\n   400\tcookie is only sent with an HTTPS connection.\n   401\t\n   402\t.. setting:: CSRF_USE_SESSIONS\n   403\t\n   404\t``CSRF_USE_SESSIONS``\n   405\t---------------------\n   406\t\n   407\tDefault: ``False``\n   408\t\n   409\tWhether to store the CSRF token in the user's session instead of in a cookie.\n   410\tIt requires the use of :mod:`django.contrib.sessions`.\n   411\t\n   412\tStoring the CSRF token in a cookie (Django's default) is safe, but storing it\n   413\tin the session is common practice in other web frameworks and therefore\n   414\tsometimes demanded by security auditors.\n   415\t\n   416\tSince the :ref:`default error views <error-views>` require the CSRF token,\n   417\t:class:`~django.contrib.sessions.middleware.SessionMiddleware` must appear in\n   418\t:setting:`MIDDLEWARE` before any middleware that may raise an exception to\n   419\ttrigger an error view (such as :exc:`~django.core.exceptions.PermissionDenied`)\n   420\tif you're using ``CSRF_USE_SESSIONS``. See :ref:`middleware-ordering`.\n   421\t\n   422\t.. setting:: CSRF_FAILURE_VIEW\n   423\t\n   424\t``CSRF_FAILURE_VIEW``\n   425\t---------------------\n   426\t\n   427\tDefault: ``'django.views.csrf.csrf_failure'``\n   428\t\n   429\tA dotted path to the view function to be used when an incoming request is\n   430\trejected by the :doc:`CSRF protection </ref/csrf>`. The function should have\n   431\tthis signature::\n   432\t\n   433\t    def csrf_failure(request, reason=\"\"):\n   434\t        ...\n   435\t\n   436\twhere ``reason`` is a short message (intended for developers or logging, not\n   437\tfor end users) indicating the reason the request was rejected. It should return\n   438\tan :class:`~django.http.HttpResponseForbidden`.\n   439\t\n   440\t``django.views.csrf.csrf_failure()`` accepts an additional ``template_name``\n   441\tparameter that defaults to ``'403_csrf.html'``. If a template with that name\n   442\texists, it will be used to render the page.\n   443\t\n   444\t.. setting:: CSRF_HEADER_NAME\n   445\t\n   446\t``CSRF_HEADER_NAME``\n   447\t--------------------\n   448\t\n   449\tDefault: ``'HTTP_X_CSRFTOKEN'``\n   450\t\n   451\tThe name of the request header used for CSRF authentication.\n   452\t\n   453\tAs with other HTTP headers in ``request.META``, the header name received from\n   454\tthe server is normalized by converting all characters to uppercase, replacing\n   455\tany hyphens with underscores, and adding an ``'HTTP_'`` prefix to the name.\n   456\tFor example, if your client sends a ``'X-XSRF-TOKEN'`` header, the setting\n   457\tshould be ``'HTTP_X_XSRF_TOKEN'``.\n   458\t\n   459\t.. setting:: CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS\n   460\t\n   461\t``CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS``\n   462\t------------------------\n   463\t\n   464\tDefault: ``[]`` (Empty list)\n   465\t\n   466\tA list of trusted origins for unsafe requests (e.g. ``POST``).\n   467\t\n   468\tFor requests that include the ``Origin`` header, Django's CSRF protection\n   469\trequires that header match the origin present in the ``Host`` header.\n   470\t\n   471\tFor a :meth:`secure <django.http.HttpRequest.is_secure>` unsafe\n   472\trequest that doesn't include the ``Origin`` header, the request must have a\n   473\t``Referer`` header that matches the origin present in the ``Host`` header.\n   474\t\n   475\tThese checks prevent, for example, a ``POST`` request from\n   476\t``subdomain.example.com`` from succeeding against ``api.example.com``. If you\n   477\tneed cross-origin unsafe requests, continuing the example, add\n   478\t``'https://subdomain.example.com'`` to this list (and/or ``http://...`` if\n   479\trequests originate from an insecure page).\n   480\t\n   481\tThe setting also supports subdomains, so you could add\n   482\t``'https://*.example.com'``, for example, to allow access from all subdomains\n   483\tof ``example.com``.\n   484\t\n   485\t.. versionchanged:: 4.0\n   486\t\n   487\t    The values in older versions must only include the hostname (possibly with\n   488\t    a leading dot) and not the scheme or an asterisk.\n   489\t\n   490\t    Also, ``Origin`` header checking isn't performed in older versions.\n   491\t\n   492\t.. setting:: DATABASES\n   493\t\n   494\t``DATABASES``\n   495\t-------------\n   496\t\n   497\tDefault: ``{}`` (Empty dictionary)\n   498\t\n   499\tA dictionary containing the settings for all databases to be used with\n   500\tDjango. It is a nested dictionary whose contents map a database alias\n   501\tto a dictionary containing the options for an individual database.\n   502\t\n   503\tThe :setting:`DATABASES` setting must configure a ``default`` database;\n   504\tany number of additional databases may also be specified.\n   505\t\n   506\tThe simplest possible settings file is for a single-database setup using\n   507\tSQLite. This can be configured using the following::\n   508\t\n   509\t    DATABASES = {\n   510\t        'default': {\n   511\t            'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3',\n   512\t            'NAME': 'mydatabase',\n   513\t        }\n   514\t    }\n   515\t\n   516\tWhen connecting to other database backends, such as MariaDB, MySQL, Oracle, or\n   517\tPostgreSQL, additional connection parameters will be required. See\n   518\tthe :setting:`ENGINE <DATABASE-ENGINE>` setting below on how to specify\n   519\tother database types. This example is for PostgreSQL::\n   520\t\n   521\t    DATABASES = {\n   522\t        'default': {\n   523\t            'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.postgresql',\n   524\t            'NAME': 'mydatabase',\n   525\t            'USER': 'mydatabaseuser',\n   526\t            'PASSWORD': 'mypassword',\n   527\t            'HOST': '127.0.0.1',\n   528\t            'PORT': '5432',\n   529\t        }\n   530\t    }\n   531\t\n   532\tThe following inner options that may be required for more complex\n   533\tconfigurations are available:\n   534\t\n   535\t.. setting:: DATABASE-ATOMIC_REQUESTS\n   536\t\n   537\t``ATOMIC_REQUESTS``\n   538\t~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   539\t\n   540\tDefault: ``False``\n   541\t\n   542\tSet this to ``True`` to wrap each view in a transaction on this database. See\n   543\t:ref:`tying-transactions-to-http-requests`.\n   544\t\n   545\t.. setting:: DATABASE-AUTOCOMMIT\n   546\t\n   547\t``AUTOCOMMIT``\n   548\t~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   549\t\n   550\tDefault: ``True``\n   551\t\n   552\tSet this to ``False`` if you want to :ref:`disable Django's transaction\n   553\tmanagement <deactivate-transaction-management>` and implement your own.\n   554\t\n   555\t.. setting:: DATABASE-ENGINE\n   556\t\n   557\t``ENGINE``\n   558\t~~~~~~~~~~\n   559\t\n   560\tDefault: ``''`` (Empty string)\n   561\t\n   562\tThe database backend to use. The built-in database backends are:\n   563\t\n   564\t* ``'django.db.backends.postgresql'``\n   565\t* ``'django.db.backends.mysql'``\n   566\t* ``'django.db.backends.sqlite3'``\n   567\t* ``'django.db.backends.oracle'``\n   568\t\n   569\tYou can use a database backend that doesn't ship with Django by setting\n   570\t``ENGINE`` to a fully-qualified path (i.e. ``mypackage.backends.whatever``).\n   571\t\n   572\t.. setting:: HOST\n   573\t\n   574\t``HOST``\n   575\t~~~~~~~~\n   576\t\n   577\tDefault: ``''`` (Empty string)\n   578\t\n   579\tWhich host to use when connecting to the database. An empty string means\n   580\tlocalhost. Not used with SQLite.\n   581\t\n   582\tIf this value starts with a forward slash (``'/'``) and you're using MySQL,\n   583\tMySQL will connect via a Unix socket to the specified socket. For example::\n   584\t\n   585\t    \"HOST\": '/var/run/mysql'\n   586\t\n   587\tIf you're using MySQL and this value *doesn't* start with a forward slash, then\n   588\tthis value is assumed to be the host.\n   589\t\n   590\tIf you're using PostgreSQL, by default (empty :setting:`HOST`), the connection\n   591\tto the database is done through UNIX domain sockets ('local' lines in\n   592\t``pg_hba.conf``). If your UNIX domain socket is not in the standard location,\n   593\tuse the same value of ``unix_socket_directory`` from ``postgresql.conf``.\n   594\tIf you want to connect through TCP sockets, set :setting:`HOST` to 'localhost'\n   595\tor '127.0.0.1' ('host' lines in ``pg_hba.conf``).\n   596\tOn Windows, you should always define :setting:`HOST`, as UNIX domain sockets\n   597\tare not available.\n   598\t\n   599\t.. setting:: NAME\n   600\t\n   601\t``NAME``\n   602\t~~~~~~~~\n   603\t\n   604\tDefault: ``''`` (Empty string)\n   605\t\n   606\tThe name of the database to use. For SQLite, it's the full path to the database\n   607\tfile. When specifying the path, always use forward slashes, even on Windows\n   608\t(e.g. ``C:/homes/user/mysite/sqlite3.db``).\n   609\t\n   610\t.. setting:: CONN_MAX_AGE\n   611\t\n   612\t``CONN_MAX_AGE``\n   613\t~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   614\t\n   615\tDefault: ``0``\n   616\t\n   617\tThe lifetime of a database connection, as an integer of seconds. Use ``0`` to\n   618\tclose database connections at the end of each request — Django's historical\n   619\tbehavior — and ``None`` for unlimited persistent connections.\n   620\t\n   621\t.. setting:: OPTIONS\n   622\t\n   623\t``OPTIONS``\n   624\t~~~~~~~~~~~\n   625\t\n   626\tDefault: ``{}`` (Empty dictionary)\n   627\t\n   628\tExtra parameters to use when connecting to the database. Available parameters\n   629\tvary depending on your database backend.\n   630\t\n   631\tSome information on available parameters can be found in the\n   632\t:doc:`Database Backends </ref/databases>` documentation. For more information,\n   633\tconsult your backend module's own documentation.\n   634\t\n   635\t.. setting:: PASSWORD\n   636\t\n   637\t``PASSWORD``\n   638\t~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   639\t\n   640\tDefault: ``''`` (Empty string)\n   641\t\n   642\tThe password to use when connecting to the database. Not used with SQLite.\n   643\t\n   644\t.. setting:: PORT\n   645\t\n   646\t``PORT``\n   647\t~~~~~~~~\n   648\t\n   649\tDefault: ``''`` (Empty string)\n   650\t\n   651\tThe port to use when connecting to the database. An empty string means the\n   652\tdefault port. Not used with SQLite.\n   653\t\n   654\t.. setting:: DATABASE-TIME_ZONE\n   655\t\n   656\t``TIME_ZONE``\n   657\t~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   658\t\n   659\tDefault: ``None``\n   660\t\n   661\tA string representing the time zone for this database connection or ``None``.\n   662\tThis inner option of the :setting:`DATABASES` setting accepts the same values\n   663\tas the general :setting:`TIME_ZONE` setting.\n   664\t\n   665\tWhen :setting:`USE_TZ` is ``True`` and this option is set, reading datetimes\n   666\tfrom the database returns aware datetimes in this time zone instead of UTC.\n   667\tWhen :setting:`USE_TZ` is ``False``, it is an error to set this option.\n   668\t\n   669\t* If the database backend doesn't support time zones (e.g. SQLite, MySQL,\n   670\t  Oracle), Django reads and writes datetimes in local time according to this\n   671\t  option if it is set and in UTC if it isn't.\n   672\t\n   673\t  Changing the connection time zone changes how datetimes are read from and\n   674\t  written to the database.\n   675\t\n   676\t  * If Django manages the database and you don't have a strong reason to do\n   677\t    otherwise, you should leave this option unset. It's best to store datetimes\n   678\t    in UTC because it avoids ambiguous or nonexistent datetimes during daylight\n   679\t    saving time changes. Also, receiving datetimes in UTC keeps datetime\n   680\t    arithmetic simple — there's no need for the ``normalize()`` method provided\n   681\t    by pytz.\n   682\t\n   683\t  * If you're connecting to a third-party database that stores datetimes in a\n   684\t    local time rather than UTC, then you must set this option to the\n   685\t    appropriate time zone. Likewise, if Django manages the database but\n   686\t    third-party systems connect to the same database and expect to find\n   687\t    datetimes in local time, then you must set this option.\n   688\t\n   689\t* If the database backend supports time zones (e.g. PostgreSQL), the\n   690\t  ``TIME_ZONE`` option is very rarely needed. It can be changed at any time;\n   691\t  the database takes care of converting datetimes to the desired time zone.\n   692\t\n   693\t  Setting the time zone of the database connection may be useful for running\n   694\t  raw SQL queries involving date/time functions provided by the database, such\n   695\t  as ``date_trunc``, because their results depend on the time zone.\n   696\t\n   697\t  However, this has a downside: receiving all datetimes in local time makes\n   698\t  datetime arithmetic more tricky — you must call the ``normalize()`` method\n   699\t  provided by pytz after each operation.\n   700\t\n   701\t  Consider converting to local time explicitly with ``AT TIME ZONE`` in raw SQL\n   702\t  queries instead of setting the ``TIME_ZONE`` option.\n   703\t\n   704\t.. setting:: DATABASE-DISABLE_SERVER_SIDE_CURSORS\n   705\t\n   706\t``DISABLE_SERVER_SIDE_CURSORS``\n   707\t~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   708\t\n   709\tDefault: ``False``\n   710\t\n   711\tSet this to ``True`` if you want to disable the use of server-side cursors with\n   712\t:meth:`.QuerySet.iterator`. :ref:`transaction-pooling-server-side-cursors`\n   713\tdescribes the use case.\n   714\t\n   715\tThis is a PostgreSQL-specific setting.\n   716\t\n   717\t.. setting:: USER\n   718\t\n   719\t``USER``\n   720\t~~~~~~~~\n   721\t\n   722\tDefault: ``''`` (Empty string)\n   723\t\n   724\tThe username to use when connecting to the database. Not used with SQLite.\n   725\t\n   726\t.. setting:: DATABASE-TEST\n   727\t\n   728\t``TEST``\n   729\t~~~~~~~~\n   730\t\n   731\tDefault: ``{}`` (Empty dictionary)\n   732\t\n   733\tA dictionary of settings for test databases; for more details about the\n   734\tcreation and use of test databases, see :ref:`the-test-database`.\n   735\t\n   736\tHere's an example with a test database configuration::\n   737\t\n   738\t    DATABASES = {\n   739\t        'default': {\n   740\t            'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.postgresql',\n   741\t            'USER': 'mydatabaseuser',\n   742\t            'NAME': 'mydatabase',\n   743\t            'TEST': {\n   744\t                'NAME': 'mytestdatabase',\n   745\t            },\n   746\t        },\n   747\t    }\n   748\t\n   749\tThe following keys in the ``TEST`` dictionary are available:\n   750\t\n   751\t.. setting:: TEST_CHARSET\n   752\t\n   753\t``CHARSET``\n   754\t^^^^^^^^^^^\n   755\t\n   756\tDefault: ``None``\n   757\t\n   758\tThe character set encoding used to create the test database. The value of this\n   759\tstring is passed directly through to the database, so its format is\n   760\tbackend-specific.\n   761\t\n   762\tSupported by the PostgreSQL_ (``postgresql``) and MySQL_ (``mysql``) backends.\n   763\t\n   764\t.. _PostgreSQL: https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/multibyte.html\n   765\t.. _MySQL: https://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/en/charset-charsets.html\n   766\t\n   767\t.. setting:: TEST_COLLATION\n   768\t\n   769\t``COLLATION``\n   770\t^^^^^^^^^^^^^\n   771\t\n   772\tDefault: ``None``\n   773\t\n   774\tThe collation order to use when creating the test database. This value is\n   775\tpassed directly to the backend, so its format is backend-specific.\n   776\t\n   777\tOnly supported for the ``mysql`` backend (see the `MySQL manual`_ for details).\n   778\t\n   779\t.. _MySQL manual: MySQL_\n   780\t\n   781\t.. setting:: TEST_DEPENDENCIES\n   782\t\n   783\t``DEPENDENCIES``\n   784\t^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^\n   785\t\n   786\tDefault: ``['default']``, for all databases other than ``default``,\n   787\twhich has no dependencies.\n   788\t\n   789\tThe creation-order dependencies of the database. See the documentation\n   790\ton :ref:`controlling the creation order of test databases\n   791\t<topics-testing-creation-dependencies>` for details.\n   792\t\n   793\t.. setting:: TEST_MIGRATE\n   794\t\n   795\t``MIGRATE``\n   796\t^^^^^^^^^^^\n   797\t\n   798\tDefault: ``True``\n   799\t\n   800\tWhen set to ``False``, migrations won't run when creating the test database.\n   801\tThis is similar to setting ``None`` as a value in :setting:`MIGRATION_MODULES`,\n   802\tbut for all apps.\n   803\t\n   804\t.. setting:: TEST_MIRROR\n   805\t\n   806\t``MIRROR``\n   807\t^^^^^^^^^^\n   808\t\n   809\tDefault: ``None``\n   810\t\n   811\tThe alias of the database that this database should mirror during\n   812\ttesting.\n   813\t\n   814\tThis setting exists to allow for testing of primary/replica\n   815\t(referred to as master/slave by some databases)\n   816\tconfigurations of multiple databases. See the documentation on\n   817\t:ref:`testing primary/replica configurations\n   818\t<topics-testing-primaryreplica>` for details.\n   819\t\n   820\t.. setting:: TEST_NAME\n   821\t\n   822\t``NAME``\n   823\t^^^^^^^^\n   824\t\n   825\tDefault: ``None``\n   826\t\n   827\tThe name of database to use when running the test suite.\n   828\t\n   829\tIf the default value (``None``) is used with the SQLite database engine, the\n   830\ttests will use a memory resident database. For all other database engines the\n   831\ttest database will use the name ``'test_' + DATABASE_NAME``.\n   832\t\n   833\tSee :ref:`the-test-database`.\n   834\t\n   835\t.. setting:: TEST_SERIALIZE\n   836\t\n   837\t``SERIALIZE``\n   838\t^^^^^^^^^^^^^\n   839\t\n   840\tBoolean value to control whether or not the default test runner serializes the\n   841\tdatabase into an in-memory JSON string before running tests (used to restore\n   842\tthe database state between tests if you don't have transactions). You can set\n   843\tthis to ``False`` to speed up creation time if you don't have any test classes\n   844\twith :ref:`serialized_rollback=True <test-case-serialized-rollback>`.\n   845\t\n   846\t.. deprecated:: 4.0\n   847\t\n   848\t   This setting is deprecated as it can be inferred from the\n   849\t   :attr:`~django.test.TestCase.databases` with the\n   850\t   :ref:`serialized_rollback <test-case-serialized-rollback>` option enabled.\n   851\t\n   852\t.. setting:: TEST_TEMPLATE\n   853\t\n   854\t``TEMPLATE``\n   855\t^^^^^^^^^^^^\n   856\t\n   857\tThis is a PostgreSQL-specific setting.\n   858\t\n   859\tThe name of a `template`_ (e.g. ``'template0'``) from which to create the test\n   860\tdatabase.\n   861\t\n   862\t.. _template: https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/sql-createdatabase.html\n   863\t\n   864\t.. setting:: TEST_CREATE\n   865\t\n   866\t``CREATE_DB``\n   867\t^^^^^^^^^^^^^\n   868\t\n   869\tDefault: ``True``\n   870\t\n   871\tThis is an Oracle-specific setting.\n   872\t\n   873\tIf it is set to ``False``, the test tablespaces won't be automatically created\n   874\tat the beginning of the tests or dropped at the end.\n   875\t\n   876\t.. setting:: TEST_USER_CREATE\n   877\t\n   878\t``CREATE_USER``\n   879\t^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^\n   880\t\n   881\tDefault: ``True``\n   882\t\n   883\tThis is an Oracle-specific setting.\n   884\t\n   885\tIf it is set to ``False``, the test user won't be automatically created at the\n   886\tbeginning of the tests and dropped at the end.\n   887\t\n   888\t.. setting:: TEST_USER\n   889\t\n   890\t``USER``\n   891\t^^^^^^^^\n   892\t\n   893\tDefault: ``None``\n   894\t\n   895\tThis is an Oracle-specific setting.\n   896\t\n   897\tThe username to use when connecting to the Oracle database that will be used\n   898\twhen running tests. If not provided, Django will use ``'test_' + USER``.\n   899\t\n   900\t.. setting:: TEST_PASSWD\n   901\t\n   902\t``PASSWORD``\n   903\t^^^^^^^^^^^^\n   904\t\n   905\tDefault: ``None``\n   906\t\n   907\tThis is an Oracle-specific setting.\n   908\t\n   909\tThe password to use when connecting to the Oracle database that will be used\n   910\twhen running tests. If not provided, Django will generate a random password.\n   911\t\n   912\t.. setting:: TEST_ORACLE_MANAGED_FILES\n   913\t\n   914\t``ORACLE_MANAGED_FILES``\n   915\t^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^\n   916\t\n   917\tDefault: ``False``\n   918\t\n   919\tThis is an Oracle-specific setting.\n   920\t\n   921\tIf set to ``True``, Oracle Managed Files (OMF) tablespaces will be used.\n   922\t:setting:`DATAFILE` and :setting:`DATAFILE_TMP` will be ignored.\n   923\t\n   924\t.. setting:: TEST_TBLSPACE\n   925\t\n   926\t``TBLSPACE``\n   927\t^^^^^^^^^^^^\n   928\t\n   929\tDefault: ``None``\n   930\t\n   931\tThis is an Oracle-specific setting.\n   932\t\n   933\tThe name of the tablespace that will be used when running tests. If not\n   934\tprovided, Django will use ``'test_' + USER``.\n   935\t\n   936\t.. setting:: TEST_TBLSPACE_TMP\n   937\t\n   938\t``TBLSPACE_TMP``\n   939\t^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^\n   940\t\n   941\tDefault: ``None``\n   942\t\n   943\tThis is an Oracle-specific setting.\n   944\t\n   945\tThe name of the temporary tablespace that will be used when running tests. If\n   946\tnot provided, Django will use ``'test_' + USER + '_temp'``.\n   947\t\n   948\t.. setting:: DATAFILE\n   949\t\n   950\t``DATAFILE``\n   951\t^^^^^^^^^^^^\n   952\t\n   953\tDefault: ``None``\n   954\t\n   955\tThis is an Oracle-specific setting.\n   956\t\n   957\tThe name of the datafile to use for the TBLSPACE.
